races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Race

runners.name: Neolithic
runners.spotlight: Stepped up from his first two runs with a 6f win at Naas last July when wearing first-time cheekpieces; a revelation on his reappearance in the Gladness Stakes at the Curragh 11 days ago; belied his 28-1 odds (stablemate Albert Einstein 11-10 fav) with a prominent run, stuck to his task to finish third, splitting older horses rated 109 and 112.
runners.comment: 6f winner at two, gave a lively display in the Gladness, sets a high standard here
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Controlled
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 5f maiden at Naas last September on the second of three starts for Aidan O'Brien; finished last of eight, weakening from 1f out, in a 7f Listed event at Dundalk in October; provided reassurance that he stays this far when beaten only a head by the Ballydoyle-trained Causeway in the Madrid Handicap at Naas on debut for Danny Murphy.
runners.comment: Beaten only a head in the Madrid Handicap, 10lb behind Neolithic on official figures
runners.owner: W D Gannon & Paul M OLoughlin

runners.name: Morehampton
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the second attempt in a C&D maiden last August (good); fair third in a rated race over an extended 7f at Roscommon; failed to get a clear run in a 6f Listed race at Fairyhouse, and his sixth placing can be upgraded; never got in a blow when joint-favourite for the 6f Birdcatcher Nursery.
runners.comment: C&D maiden winner, faces a tough task against Controlled and Neolithic at these weights

runners.name: Greek Mythology
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the useful Atlantic Coast, a 7f Listed winner for this stable before continuing his career in Australia; looked a useful prospect when making all to win a median auction race over this trip at Dundalk in December; tongue-tie worn then is retained.
runners.comment: Debut winner over this trip at Dundalk in December, can continue to pay his way

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Bobby McGee
runners.spotlight: The best of four runs last year was when a close third in a 6f Curragh maiden; that came on good ground; also ran well over 7f previously; gelded since; rated 81 and should be a big player if handling this ground.
runners.comment: Best run last year came at 6f but has run well at this trip too; player if handling ground

runners.name: Jamestown
runners.spotlight: Posted a solid effort on debut over this trip at Dundalk recently; there should be improvement to come and should be involved today if handling the different conditions.
runners.comment: Solid effort on debut over this trip at Dundalk; be involved if handling these conditions

runners.name: Kilmac Air
runners.spotlight: Second foal; sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Elliott (RPR 62); dam US Listed-placed 1m/8.5f winner (90), half-sister to 6f Listed winner Lustown Baba, out of 5f 2yo winner; Eur50,000 yearling comes from a speedy family though dam stayed 1m; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Comes from a speedy family though dam stayed 1m; worth a market check
runners.owner: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs M V Magnier

runners.name: Harmani
runners.spotlight: The best of two runs last year came on debut at Navan over 6f; seemed not to stay 7f 110yds at Roscommon next time; however, that was 11 months ago and will be a stronger horse now so this trip should be fine; a player.
runners.comment: Seemed not to stay 7f 110yds last run but has matured since; should be a player

runners.name: Tavarua
runners.spotlight: Finished 8th in a barrier trial at Naas last May when with Kevin Coleman; now with a different yard; best watched for future reference.
runners.comment: Finished 8th in a barrier trial at Naas last May when with Kevin Coleman; best watched

runners.name: Absoluteassignment
runners.spotlight: Third foal; brother to 1m 2yo winner Runninsonofagun (RPR 70); dam unraced half-sister to winners Lady Quickstep (5f/6f inc 2yo) and Dreams Delivered (6f-1m inc 2yo); related to winners but likely to improve for this.
runners.comment: Related to winners but likely to improve for this

runners.name: Satoyama
runners.spotlight: One of two for the yard; Eur26,000 yearling is half-brother to German winners Suada (Group-placed 1m 2yo; RPR 102), Sibelius (Listed-placed 8.5f; 101) and Sequel (5f-6.5f); dam 8.4f winner (75); related to some decent sorts; check market.
runners.comment: One of two for the yard; Eur26,000 yearling is related to some decent sorts; check market

runners.name: Shaws Bay
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-brother to 7f AW winner Profitar Rules (RPR 51); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f/1m6f Listed winner Sirrin and to 7f 2yo winner Sinaniya (dam of 6f Group 3 winner Sonaiyla); Eur20,000 yearling comes from a decent damline and could run well on debut.
runners.comment: Eur20,000 yearling comes from a decent damline and could run well on debut

runners.name: Windsor Lily
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far on second Dundalk start over 6f; needs loads of improvement under much different conditions.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on second Dundalk start over 6f; needs loads of improvement
runners.owner: Shane R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Sweet Baby Zou
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; always behind when beaten 13l in a 1m2f Navan maiden on recent debut; this trip may suit better but still has to do a lot more.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l in a 1m2f Navan maiden on recent debut; this trip may suit better

runners.name: Mr Perth
runners.spotlight: Slowly away when beaten 13l over this trip at the Curragh on debut; has to improve loads.
runners.comment: Slowly away when beaten 13l over this trip at the Curragh on debut; has to improve loads

runners.name: Freedom And Light
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l on debut over 1m at Dundalk last November; will have to improve plenty for these different conditions.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l on debut over 1m at Dundalk last November; will have to improve plenty

runners.name: Oh I Do Declare
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in testing ground at Listowel last September; best watched today.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in testing ground at Listowel last September; best watched today

races.name: Gowran Park Handicap

runners.name: Cisterna
runners.spotlight: Moderate form overall in five nursery runs, although a Cork second was encouraging; creditable fifth over 5f at Naas on latest; however, rider deserts him to partner Aurora Mist.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth over 5f at Naas on latest, rider deserts him to partner Aurora Mist

runners.name: Beau Army
runners.spotlight: Winner of two low-grade handicaps at Dundalk; limited turf experience early in career;
runners.comment: Winner of two low-grade handicaps at Dundalk, limited turf experience early in career

runners.name: Unfamiliar
runners.spotlight: Three unplaced runs at Dundalk; stayed on in mid-field over 6f on handicap/turf debut at Naas; may do better now.
runners.comment: Stayed on in mid-field over 6f on handicap/turf debut at Naas, may do better now

runners.name: Monocle Rocket
runners.spotlight: Ran three times at two, twice finished last; not a bad run on middle start; new yard now; hard to be confident.
runners.comment: Ran three times at two, twice finished last, not a bad run on middle start,  new yard now

runners.name: Aurora Mist
runners.spotlight: Some merit in her nursery form; signed off with a fourth placing in a 15-runner event over this trip on heavy ground at Galway;  stable's excellent current form is a plus.
runners.comment: Good run on heavy at Galway on final 2yo start, stable's excellent current form is a plus

runners.name: Survivors Code
runners.spotlight: Two 7f nursery outings were respectable; appeared not to stay 1m2f on reappearance/stable debut at Navan.
runners.comment: Two 7f nursery outings were respectable, appeared not to stay 1m2f on reappearance

runners.name: Dream And Believe
runners.spotlight: Has finished seventh in sprint handicaps at Naas and Dundalk; not beaten far in each case, but clearly needs to find a bit extra; cheekpieces for the first time.
runners.comment: Has finished seventh in two sprint handicaps, not beaten far but needs to find extra
runners.owner: Kildare Racing Synd/Mrs John Murphy/Kilmichael Racing Synd

runners.name: Nephin Mountain
runners.spotlight: Not an obvious contender based on three turf outings at two; trainer also saddles the top weight.
runners.comment: Not an obvious contender based on three turf outings at two, trainer also has top weight

runners.name: Andosky
runners.spotlight: Fair fifth in a C&D maiden last July on good ground; best effort when second in a 6f Sligo nursery; never in the hunt over the same trip at Navan on her final juvenile start.
runners.comment: Fair fifth in a C&D maiden last July, best effort when second in a 6f Sligo nursery

runners.name: Lilo Pelekai
runners.spotlight: Weak form in maidens; finished in front of only one rival in nursery starts at Galway and Dundalk.
runners.comment: Weak form in maidens, finished in front of only one rival in two nursery starts

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Rayzera
runners.spotlight: Looked in need of further when 9l fifth of ten over 7f here last September; appreciated the extra furlong when 5l fifth of 16 over this trip at Cork later that month; open to improvement; might come into his own over a bit further than this.
runners.comment: Fair fifth of 16 over this trip at Cork, might come into his own over a bit further
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Sinmara
runners.spotlight: Kingman filly; 400,000gns yearling; closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Spinto Soprano, half-sister to 1m AW winner/St Leger runner-up Rahiebb; always in touch when a promising third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies in a 7f maiden on soft-to-heavy at the Curragh on only start at two.
runners.comment: Promising third behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained fillies on debut, strong contender

runners.name: Croi Gra
runners.spotlight: Alkumait half-sister to four winners including Holiday Magic (ten wins, all on AW), Coolnagree (two 7f wins, Group 3-placed) and Blackhall (point/4m Maryland Hunt Cup); dam placed 2m hurdle, half-sister to HK/Singapore 6f Group 1 winner Lucky Nine; hooded for debut.
runners.comment: Alkumait half-sister to four winners with a very varied profile, hooded for debut
runners.owner: Gary Devlin & Kevin Osborne & Richard J Fitzsimons

runners.name: Wedding
runners.spotlight: E350,000 yearling; fourth foal; No Nay Never filly is a sister to winners Broadhurst (7f-1m2f RPR 112) and Love Comedy (Japanese 6f turf 2yo/7f dirt); dam French/US 1m-10.5f winner (inc Listed; 100); of obvious interest, even though she holds no big-race entries.
runners.comment: E350,000 yearling, No Nay Never filly is a sister to two winners, unlikely to be far away
runners.owner: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith

runners.name: Retro Gal
runners.spotlight: Two modest efforts at two after running in a barrier trial; started slowly when well beaten over C&D on debut at this venue; again started slowly when failing to improve in a 1m Dundalk maiden.
runners.comment: Well beaten over C&D on debut at this venue, failed to improve in a 1m Dundalk maiden

runners.name: Remember That
runners.spotlight: Calyx filly is half-sister to seven winners, notably the top-class Rip Van Winkle; ran green, never counted on debut in a 7f Naas fillies' maiden last August; raced keenly and dropped away in the last furlong of a similar race at Galway; has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the top-class Rip Van Winkle, needs to step up on her juvenile form
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Valentinas Grace
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always behind on only Dundalk start; has changed stable; best watched.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always behind on only Dundalk start, has changed stable, best watched

races.name: Golf At Gowran Park Claiming Race

runners.name: Incredible Army
runners.spotlight: Won a three-runner 6f race at Dundalk in February; modest overall form on AW; makes turf debut; others preferred.
runners.comment: Won a three-runner 6f race at Dundalk in February, modest overall form on AW, turf debut

runners.name: Kactus Jack
runners.spotlight: Made a promising introduction in a Naas maiden last September; failed to build on that in two subsequent turf outings; fair fourth of nine in a 7f race of this type on his first outing this year; never counted in a 1m handicap at the same venue.
runners.comment: Can be given a chance on the form of his fourth in a claimer on AW on penultimate start

runners.name: Coincidental Glory
runners.spotlight: Signed off for 2025 with a Dundalk win after several promising efforts; good chance here, though worth bearing in mind he was a beaten favourite three times on the AW before his breakthrough success; an unknown quantity on the prevailing soft ground; Sam Coen's claim a help.
runners.comment: Signed off for 2025 with a Dundalk win after several promising efforts, good chance here
runners.owner: Total Recall Racing Club/J Fairhurst/N OHare/Kabin Racing

runners.name: Clonbun
runners.spotlight: Possibly flattered by an official rating (68) that gives him a fair chance in theory; blinkered for the first time.
runners.comment: Possibly flattered by an official rating (68) that gives him a fair chance in theory

runners.name: Green Testimony
runners.spotlight: Well held in two strong turf maidens last term; Cork race in which she was 13l seventh of 11 was won by subsequent dual Group 1 winner Precise; should be better for a run in a 6f maiden at Dundalk and this trip should be more suitable; interesting in this grade with Julian Pietropaolo claiming 7lb.
runners.comment: Ran in two 7f turf maidens at two, should be better for a run in a 6f maiden at Dundalk

runners.name: Balance Of Trade
runners.spotlight: Ran well on the first of two nursery outings, finishing fourth of 15 over 7f at this venue; appeared not to stay 1m2f on seasonal debut at Navan.
runners.comment: Fair fourth over 7f at this venue in a nursery, appeared not to stay 1m2f at Navan

runners.name: Areyousure
runners.spotlight: Has been given a modest handicap rating (48) after three starts, up against it in this company.
runners.comment: Has been given a modest handicap rating after three starts, up against it in this company

runners.name: Femme Beauty
runners.spotlight: Showed mild promise in maidens and over 7f at Down Royal in her first nursery; regressive later in the season; down the field on seasonal debut; 10lb claimer aboard here with a senior rider on stablemate Captive Beauty.
runners.comment: Regressive in a busy juvenile campaign, down the field on seasonal debut, no appeal

runners.name: Captive Beauty
runners.spotlight: Failed to make much impression over 6f at Cork on only start at two, open to improvement with a tongue-tie added; looks the pick of Ken Condon's pair.
runners.comment: Failed to make much impression over 6f at Cork on only start at two, open to improvement
runners.owner: David K Kelly & Mrs Pauline Condon

races.name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap

runners.name: Angelight
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; got off the mark when winning a Dundalk maiden over 1m last November; had previously run well on turf and at this track; wide draw but one to consider.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner; had previously run well on turf and at this track; one to consider

runners.name: Ob La Di
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m2f at Naas last August; not as good three times since and will probably find this trip sharp on return.
runners.comment: Won over 1m2f at Naas last August; not as good since; will probably find this trip sharp

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Won over the extended 1m1f at this track last September and two decent efforts in defeat after over 1m; was out of the handicap when well beaten in the Irish Lincoln; this is more suitable.
runners.comment: Was out of the handicap when well beaten in the Irish Lincoln; this is more suitable

runners.name: Minaun View
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well in defeat several times for former yards up to 1m; was poor in a Dundalk claimer when last seen; needs a return to best for new stable.
runners.comment: Was poor in a Dundalk claimer when last seen; needs a return to best for new stable

runners.name: Ardeur
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f maiden for Ralph Beckett and later some decent efforts in defeat in France up to 1m2f; below form over various trips for this yard last autumn; others preferred.
runners.comment: Below form over various trips for this yard last autumn; others preferred

runners.name: Think About It
runners.spotlight: Best run last year was in a Limerick maiden over 1m; soundly beaten at Dundalk on debut for this yard; others preferred.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at Dundalk on debut for this yard; others preferred

runners.name: La Tulipe Noire
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Cork and Roscommon last autumn over approx' 1m; solid runs in defeat after; well beaten by Electric Beauty on return at the Curragh but can do better today.
runners.comment: Well beaten by Electric Beauty on return at the Curragh but can do better today

runners.name: Greydreambeliever
runners.spotlight: Won over 6f on debut for Karl Burke as a 2yo but didn't win since, albeit ran well several times up to 7f and on soft; bought by currentg connections for 30,000gns at the sales last autumn; possibly the yard's selected runner so watch the market.
runners.comment: Bought for 30,000gns at the sales; looks the yard's selected runner so watch the market
runners.owner: K Blake/Mrs E Palmer/M Wilson/N Luck

runners.name: Lady Odette
runners.spotlight: Best run for Ollie Sangster was when runner-up in a 1m3f Southwell novice in March last year; poor when last seen and is best watched on return for this yard.
runners.comment: Second in a 1m3f Southwell novice in March last year; poor when last seen; best watched

runners.name: Spanish Temptress
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive handicaps last October at Killarney (8f) and Roscommon (7.5f); struggled the last twice having shot up the handicap and ideally needs to drop back down a bit.
runners.comment: Struggled last twice having shot up the handicap and ideally needs to drop back down a bit

runners.name: Fast Tara
runners.spotlight: Just 1-22 but ran some fine races in defeat last year over 7f-8f; is excused a below par run when last seen; should be competitive if near her best on return.
runners.comment: Excused a below par run when last seen; should be competitive if near her best on return

runners.name: Rocket Robyn
runners.spotlight: Best run came when a close second in a 1m1.5f maiden at this track last autumn; well below that level since over 1m4f and 7f; needs a return to her best on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Second in a 1m1.5f maiden here last autumn; below that level twice since; needs her best

runners.name: Never Loose
runners.spotlight: Comfortably won a Galway maiden in heavy on debut last autumn over 7f; the form of that race is solid; yard is amongst the winners and ground will be fine; can be a player on return.
runners.comment: Galway maiden winner over this trip; ground will be fine; can be a player on return

runners.name: Thrifty Of Digby
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins have come at this track over 1m, the latest last September; not a bad effort at Navan when last seen; might need to drop a bit in the weights; wide draw.
runners.comment: Two wins here over 1m; might need to drop a bit in the weights; wide draw

runners.name: Electric Beauty
runners.spotlight: Won her third race when taking a 1m Curragh handicap in March and was a close second there since; a 5lb rise demands more but has to be taken seriously.
runners.comment: Just denied at Curragh last time; 5lb rise demands more but has to be taken seriously

runners.name: Sindria
runners.spotlight: One of two for the yard; won a soft-ground Leopardstown maiden over this trip for Dermot Weld last June; joined this yard after; not a bad run at Roscommon when last seen and can be involved if ready on return.
runners.comment: Not a bad run at Roscommon when last seen and can be involved if ready on return

races.name: Save 33% Buying Tickets Online Handicap

runners.name: Zipster
runners.spotlight: Won his third race when taking a Killarney handicap over 1m last July off this mark; some solid runs since and should improve from recent Curragh return; a player.
runners.comment: Won over 1m last July off this mark; some solid runs since; improve from Curragh return

runners.name: Saith Seren
runners.spotlight: Maiden has posted his best runs in defeat over 1m; not at her best at Dundalk over 1m2f last time; handles this ground; needs her best.
runners.comment: Not at her best at Dundalk over 1m2f last time; handles this ground; needs  best

runners.name: Captain Ciano
runners.spotlight: Maiden whose best turf run last year was when second in this race; decent Dundalk run in February but was tailed-off at the Curragh last time; has to rebound.
runners.comment: Second in this last year; 5th at Dundalk in February; tailed-off at the Curragh last time

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: All six wins came on the AW when trained in UK; lost his form for this yard last year but posted a cracking return second at the Curragh; well below that level next time and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Cracking return second at the Curragh; well below that level next time and has to rebound

runners.name: Ethereal Star
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; best handicap run came on final start last year when third over almost 1m4f at Killarney; her absence is a concern but a top rider is aboard.
runners.comment: Third over almost 1m4f at Killarney when last seen; her absence is a concern

runners.name: Ohmali
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over 1m at this track last September but couldn't cope with a 6lb rise in a better race at the Curragh next time; same mark today and needs more; seems to stay this trip.
runners.comment: Won over 1m at this track last September; not as good up 6lb next time;   needs more

runners.name: Shahada Ace
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a 7.5f Tipperary handicap last August but wasn't as good raised 13lb at Bellewstown next time; dropped 3lb on return but this ground is a question; yard going well.
runners.comment: Won over 7.5f last year; not as good next time; dropped 3lb on return;  ground a question

runners.name: Hexagonal
runners.spotlight: Some decent maiden runs in defeat last year including on soft and over this trip; disappointing at Dundalk on handicap bow; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added for return.
runners.comment: Disappointing at Dundalk on handicap bow; tongue-tie and cheekpieces added for return

runners.name: Secret Magician
runners.spotlight: His three wins from 48 starts came over shorter; ran well over 1m at Listowel last September but has been poor since and not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Ran well over 1m at Listowel last September but has been poor since and not easy to fancy

runners.name: Justenzia
runners.spotlight: Won a Clonmel handicap over this trip last autumn and was a fine fourth at Killarney over 1m after; didn't seem to stay 1m4f when last seen; a chance if ready; looks the stable selected of two.
runners.comment: Seemed not to stay 1m4f when last seen; chance if ready; looks the stable selected of two

runners.name: Bobby Kirova
runners.spotlight: Decent efforts on first two starts for Pat Murphy last year over further but was poor third start; handicap/seasonal debut for a new yard; worth a market look.
runners.comment: Handicap/seasonal debut for a new yard; worth a market look

runners.name: Matt Connor
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose winner scored over hurdles at Naas when last seen; won twice on the Flat over 1m2f in 2023; lost his form in this sphere last year but jockey booking takes the eye and considered.
runners.comment: Won over hurdles last time; lost his form on Flat last year but a notable rider booking

runners.name: Bruant
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose winner has been well beaten last five Flat starts; has dropped significantly in the handicap but still not easy to fancy.
runners.comment: Has dropped significantly in the handicap but still not easy to fancy

races.name: Racing Again Tomorrow Handicap

runners.name: Nancee Spain
runners.spotlight: Won a Roscommon handicap by 15l last October but was poor on return off a 14lb higher mark at the Curragh; down 1lb but questions now.
runners.comment: Poor on return off a 14lb higher mark at the Curragh; down 1lb but questions now

runners.name: Zanahiyr
runners.spotlight: Fine dual-purpose sort has 10 wins under all codes including a Bellewstown Flat handicap last August beating a below par Light Up The Dark; won the American Grand National when last seen; handles all ground; a big player.
runners.comment: Won the American Grand National when last seen; handles all ground; a big player

runners.name: Plunkett Street
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m4f Cork handicap on return last year and some good runs off higher marks after; won over hurdles in November but disqualified for a positive sample; could run well.
runners.comment: Won a 1m4f Cork handicap on return last year and some good runs off higher marks after

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles last year and later took a Limerick Flat handicap over 1m4f; however, has been poor on his last three Flat starts and has to bounce back.
runners.comment: Has been poor on his last three Flat starts and has to bounce back

runners.name: Slurricane
runners.spotlight: Won his second race on the Flat when winning over 1m4f at Galway last October; was poor over hurdles last time but has a shout if back in form.
runners.comment: Was poor over hurdles last time but has a shout if back in form

runners.name: Light Up The Dark
runners.spotlight: Two bumper wins and added a 1m5f Down Royal Flat maiden to her haul last year; below form at Bellewstown when last seen but is a big player if ready on return; the market could guide.
runners.comment: Below form when last seen but is a big player if ready on return; the market could guide

runners.name: Not Just Any Eagle
runners.spotlight: Won his fifth race on the Flat when scoring over 1m4f at Leopardstown last year; some fine runs in defeat after up to 2m; below form last two runs but a chance if at best on return.
runners.comment: Five wins; below form last two runs but a chance if at best on return

runners.name: Rich Belief
runners.spotlight: Seven wins between both codes but last one came on the Flat at Chester in 2024; would have a chance if back to the form of his Clonmel second last September.
runners.comment: Would have a chance if back to the form of his Clonmel second last September

runners.name: Harrys Legacy
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; won twice on the Flat in 2022 and some decent runs in defeat on the level and over hurdles last year; had a recent hurdle run to tee up for this; squeak.
runners.comment: Some decent runs in defeat on the Flat and over hurdles last year; had a recent hurdle run

runners.name: Katherine
runners.spotlight: Three time hurdle winner was disappointing on the Flat and hurdles when last seen and others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Was disappointing on the Flat and hurdles when last seen and others make more appeal

races.name: Ladies Day 9th May "Confined" Novice Stakes (No more than two runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race) (Div I)

runners.name: You Got To My Soul
runners.spotlight: Both runs at Southwell; promise on debut (1m) and better form when third over 1m3f in February, form which has been bolstered by the second and fourth since; leading form claims now switched to turf.
runners.comment: Latest Southwell 3rd is solid form; can do better & holds solid form claims on turf debut

runners.name: Infraad
runners.spotlight: In need of the experience when second of five in a 1m maiden at Doncaster (soft) last October; flattered by his proximity to the idling winner but it was still an encouraging start and today's extra yardage looks a big positive; leading contender.
runners.comment: Green but made a pleasing debut at Doncaster in October (1m, soft); likely big improver

runners.name: Hatteen
runners.spotlight: Last of three on debut (1m, soft) and fifth of six on his second 2yo run (8.3f, good to soft); the latter effort wasn't without promise, however, and this longer trip should be right up his street.
runners.comment: Only beat one rival in two 2yo runs but the 2nd effort was encouraging; longer trip a plus

runners.name: Mr Bollinger
runners.spotlight: $85,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Exude (Canadian 1m3f turf) and Investment Mandate (US 1m/8.5f turf); dam US 8.5f dirt winner (including stakes; RPR 109); stable in good form but 1m2f a query given his sire.
runners.comment: $85,000 yearling; half-brother to two winners out of a useful dirt winner; 1m2f a query

runners.name: Directors Cut
runners.spotlight: Two runs in November, fifth on debut (8.6f) before putting that experience to good use at Newcastle 19 days later where he finished strongest to beat 11 rivals (1m); new trip should suit and he is bred to carry on improving; the penalty does ask a stiffer question though.
runners.comment: 1m AW win on second 2yo start; new trip should suit but he has a penalty to shoulder

runners.name: Bay Royale
runners.spotlight: Got tired in the final furlong when fourth of nine in an 8.3f maiden (soft) here in October, but it was still an encouraging start to his career; the longer trip should suit and this Irish Derby entrant is open to plenty of improvement this year.
runners.comment: Promising debut here (8.3f) in October, tiring final furlong; much more to come this year

runners.name: Tripoli Flyer
runners.spotlight: Smart bumper/novice hurdle form; recorded an RPR of 141 when eighth in the County Hurdle four weeks ago; no match for Constitution Hill on his Flat debut but he should do better in this sphere at some point, probably when handicapping.
runners.comment: Smart bumper/novice hurdle form; well held behind Constitution Hill on Flat debut

runners.name: Grazeon Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Useful in bumpers but he has finished down the field in two Flat starts this year; handicaps likely to see him in a better light.
runners.comment: Bumper winner but down the field in two Flat runs; minor handicaps beckon

runners.name: That Darn Cantor
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and always behind in a 1m novice at Newmarket (good ground) in October on sole 2yo run; bred to leave that behind him in time.
runners.comment: 40-1 when tailed off at Newmarket in October (1m, good); bred to leave that well behind

races.name: Ladies Day 9th May "Confined" Novice Stakes (No more than two runs) (GBB/GBBPlus Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Knightsail
runners.spotlight: 125,000gns yearling by Camelot; beaten around 6l in his two runs in the autumn, most recently when third of seven at Goodwood (1m1f, good) in October; doesn't set a high standard on that form but this step back up in trip looks a good move and he should have more to offer this season.
runners.comment: Some promise in his two runs last autumn and he's in the mix on his return

runners.name: Olympic Charter
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; closely related to 5f AW winner Nordic Games (RPR 73), half-brother to three winners including Beautiful Love (1m/1m3f including US Grade 3; 115) and Flying Honours (7f-1m2f 2yo including Group 3; 108); dam 1m1f-11.5f winner (including Group 3; 105); yard 3-7 in novice events this year and he's respected on debut.
runners.comment: Has a smart pedigree and yard is 3-7 in novice events this year; striking debutant

runners.name: Australia Day
runners.spotlight: 60,000gns foal, 180,000gns yearling; fourth foal; gelded half-brother to winners Zenga (Listed-placed 9.4f AW; RPR 97) and Surpass (7f/7.4f 2yo; 96); dam 7f 2yo winner (85); half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Ricetta, out of half-sister to outstanding miler Kingman; plenty to like on paper and he's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: 180,000gns yearling; plenty to like on paper and he's an interesting newcomer

runners.name: Outback Legend
runners.spotlight: 80,000euros yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Maksud (Listed-placed 1m2f; RPR 107), Capel At Dawn (1m; 76) and Haajoos (Spanish 8.5f-9.5f); dam 9.4f AW winner (78), half-sister to US 7f/1m Grade 1 winner Gabby's Golden Gal; yard is in good form and interesting to see how he figures in market on debut.
runners.comment: 80,000euros yearling; yard is in good form and interesting to see how he figures in market

runners.name: Tornado Tower
runners.spotlight: 850,000gns yearling; fifth foal; brother to winners Hurricane Lane (1m-14.5f including triple Group 1; RPR 125) and Frankel's Storm (1m 2yo/1m4f; 98), half-brother to 1m4f-2m2f winner Sweet William (including Group 2; 120); dam 15.5f-2m winner (including /Listed; 103); has a striking pedigree and he needs a close look on debut.
runners.comment: 850,000gns yearling; has striking pedigree and he needs a close look on debut

runners.name: Monte A Bord
runners.spotlight: 45,000gns yearling; close second on bumper debut at Kelso (2m, good) last September and he justified favouritism in similar conditions at Newcastle the following month; this is a different test on his return but he has useful Flat pedigree and needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Bumper winner who has a useful Flat pedigree and he needs watching in market on his return
runners.owner: M McHale J Munroe D Naisbitt & S Hodges

runners.name: Qarreeb
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to two useful winners but he made a low-key start when seventh of ten at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in November; should know more this time but he needs a transformation upped in trip on his return.
runners.comment: Made a low-key start at Wolverhampton in November and he needs a transformation on return

runners.name: The Bitters
runners.spotlight: 150-1 and she finished tailed off on Newcastle debut (1m, AW) last month.
runners.comment: 150-1 and she finished tailed off on Newcastle debut (1m, AW) last month

runners.name: Plutarch
runners.spotlight: Out of a 7f Listed winner; shaped with some promise when a staying-on fourth on Wolverhampton debut (1m4f) in December, but he was tailed off on his return visit two weeks later; was never travelling in that race and has something to prove on his return.
runners.comment: Tailed off in the second of his two runs at Wolverhampton in December; opposable on return

races.name: Flat Is Back Restricted Novice Stakes (For horses in Bands B, C and D) (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Blackberry Bold
runners.spotlight: March foal; Â£50,000 yearling; second foal; half-sister to Italian 5f/6f 2yo winner Korhogo; dam 5f/5.4f winner (including 2yo; RPR 94); stable going along nicely and their first 2yo runner of the year performed with credit at Bath on Sunday; sole filly in the field.
runners.comment: Â£50,000 half-sister to a winner in Italy; dam a winning 2yo; yard going well; sole filly
runners.owner: Upton House Stud & Whitsbury Manor Stud

runners.name: Invincible Isaac
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and beaten over 15l in the Brocklesby on debut, but he lost all chance when completely blowing the start; showed clear promise with that in mind and he shouldn't be underestimated here if breaking cleanly.
runners.comment: Lost all chance at the start in the Brocklesby but clear promise mid-race; can do better

runners.name: Final Appeal
runners.spotlight: January foal; 37,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to 7f winner Kodiac Brown Bear (RPR 76); dam unplaced 1m AW 2yo (44), half-sister to Australian 1m4f Group 1 winner Sir John Hawkwood; will stay further in time but this early foal, who is his powerful stable's first 2yo runner of the year, commands plenty of respect.
runners.comment: 37,000euros half-brother to minor 7f winner; first 2yo runner of 2026 for top yard

runners.name: Greek Street
runners.spotlight: April foal; 32,000gns foal, 52,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to winners Mister Wilson (7f 2yo/8.5f Flat, RPR 92; 2m hurdle), Pietro (6f/7f including 2yo; 91), Port Winston (5f 2yo; 73) and Hidden Pearl (1m6f; 68); dam French 7f 2yo winner; stable not known for precocious juveniles but worth a market check all the same.
runners.comment: 52,000gns half-brother to four winners; dam a 7f 2yo winner; market revealing
runners.owner: C Booth P Boyle S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: Rotelle
runners.spotlight: February foal; 40,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to winners Rockymountainway (Listed-placed 7f/7.3f; RPR 98) and Smoky Mountain (7f/7.5f including AW/Spain; 92); dam Listed-placed 5f/6f winner (102); likely type on paper but the stable is having a quiet spell (2-57 since October 1).
runners.comment: 40,000gns half-brother to two winners out of a Listed-placed sprint winner; stable quiet

races.name: Four Legged Friend 26th April Handicap

runners.name: Spendmore Lane
runners.spotlight: The starts have been an issue for her but she finally got her act together when winning a 5f handicap at Chelmsford in February; followed up at Wolverhampton soon afterwards but she blew the start in her hat-trick bid at Chelmsford five days later; has a bit more to prove on turf but she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Back-to-back AW wins this year; slow start a hindrance in hat-trick bid latest; turf query

runners.name: Fiorella Princess
runners.spotlight: Still 5lb higher than for her last win but she has been running with credit on AW for new trainer in recent starts; others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Arrives on the back of some fair AW runs; others appeal as being better treated though

runners.name: Albegone
runners.spotlight: Multiple turf winner on good ground or slower; fit from AW, where he has run well in two of his three runs this year; should be in the thick of it.
runners.comment: Return to turf in his favour and he lurks on a dangerous mark; one for the shortlist
runners.owner: D B & Mrs C Lamplough & Partner

runners.name: Over Spiced
runners.spotlight: Three wins last season for Grace Harris; beaten but not discredited off this mark on her final three runs in 2025; got loose before the start of her intended reappearance last month (50-1 at the time); Rossa Ryan always a positive booking for the stable.
runners.comment: Three 5f wins last year; she needs to raise her game to defy this mark but it's possible

runners.name: Komorkis
runners.spotlight: 1-5 for Richard Fahey, the win coming in a three-runner novice at Newcastle last September (8-1 outsider of the three); sold for 1,400gns in October; improvement essential.
runners.comment: Won a 3-runner novice for R Fahey as a 3yo; sold 1,400gns since last seen; best watched

runners.name: Bang On The Bell
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts this winter; tumbled down the weights but not a solid option now returning to turf.
runners.comment: Out of sorts this winter; not a solid option now returning to turf

runners.name: Glory Fighter
runners.spotlight: Ten-time turf winner, including C&D; close third at Newcastle when last seen in February; one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D winner; close third on AW when last seen in February; high on the list

races.name: Family Funday 31st May Handicap

runners.name: Dryburgh
runners.spotlight: 0-5 for Jane Chapple-Hyam; low-key stable debut in February but he took a good step forward fitted with a hood and upped to 1m2f when third at Doncaster ten days ago; travelled strongly there and he remains open to further progress; good chance.
runners.comment: Promising third at Doncaster ten days ago (hooded first time); remains capable of better

runners.name: Port Darwin
runners.spotlight: Made a successful nursery debut at Musselburgh (1m, good) last August; not kicked on from that in four runs since, including when upped to 1m2f at Chelmsford last month; now has cheekpieces fitted.
runners.comment: Struggled since 1m turf win last summer; new cheekpieces not enough to tempt

runners.name: Extraterrestrial
runners.spotlight: Promising start to his handicap career when third of five at Wolverhampton last month (9.4f, AW); he will need more to come out on top but he is low mileage and the addition of a visor can give him a lift.
runners.comment: Promising 3rd on handicap debut (9.4f, AW); needs more but the new visor could prompt it

runners.name: Tiny Riot
runners.spotlight: The form of his latest seventh of eight at Southwell (7f) has taken some knocks and he was never the slightest threat in a steadily run affair; did plug on though and he could prove a different proposition now up in trip for his handicap debut; Dan Skelton still seeking a first Flat winner but that is unlikely to remain the case for long and Rossa Ryan has been booked; one to consider.
runners.comment: Modest form from 6f-7f but this son of Sottsass may be a different proposition over 1m2f

runners.name: Causing Problems
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three 2yo runs; fitted with a hood for his handicap/seasonal debut at Wolverhampton nine days ago, a race where he was sent off a warm favourite but pulled his chance away early; tongue-tie now added; will need to settle better now upped in class.
runners.comment: Pulled too hard when disappointing favourite on recent h'cap debut; needs to settle better

runners.name: Any Which Way
runners.spotlight: Looked a work in progress in three 2yo runs, showing his best form on the second occasion (1m, good to soft); looks sure to improve for 1m2f on paper and he returns with his stable going well.
runners.comment: Work in progress last year; likely big improver now upped in trip and handicapping

runners.name: Als River
runners.spotlight: Fair form in three quick runs last autumn, finishing third of 14 at Chelmsford on the most recent of them (1m); stamina in bottom half of his pedigree but this opening mark will demand improvement; gelded since last run.
runners.comment: Promise at 2yrs but the new trip/gelding operation not enough to tempt this time

runners.name: Femme Fatale
runners.spotlight: The second of her three 2yo runs was respectable (1m1f, good to soft) and she should have no problem with this trip and further; market to guide back from six months off.
runners.comment: One encouraging 2yo run (1m1f, good to soft); market useful handicapping after a break

runners.name: Glenna
runners.spotlight: Promise in three novice runs (7f-1m; best run on soft ground) as a 2yo; bred to appreciate the extra distance but she needs to prove this opening mark is in range.
runners.comment: Promise in three novice runs over shorter last year; new trip a plus; opening mark no gift

races.name: Â£9 Racedays At Nottingham Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Rosemarys Rose
runners.spotlight: Has done well since joining Rod Millman including two wins on Polytrack (1m) and a creditable second at Lingfield 19 days ago; on same mark as for that last run and is a big player if she can transfer her progress back to turf.
runners.comment: Two AW wins for new yard and is big player if she can transfer her progress back to turf

runners.name: Revich
runners.spotlight: All seven wins have been on good or slower ground on turf; last success was in 2023 but he didn't get much luck when fourth at Wolverhampton (7f) last month and he's in the mix on this step back up in trip.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but he didn't get much luck at Wolverhampton last month; in the mix

runners.name: Antiquity
runners.spotlight: Won on Polytrack at Lingfield (1m) in February and was fair fifth in a Class 5 event at Wolverhampton last month; remains feasibly treated on his best form and his five turf runs include a win over C&D (good) last April; respected on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has been in good form on AW this year; respected back on turf
runners.owner: P&h Moorhead J Collins And S Piper

runners.name: Gladiadora
runners.spotlight: Scored at Bath (1m, good to firm) last August and she's added two AW wins since including a brave success in a tight finish at Southwell (7f, Tapeta) 11 days ago; only 2lb higher back on turf and she's strongly respected back up in trip.
runners.comment: Back from a break with a win at Southwell 11 days ago and she's a key player back on turf
runners.owner: Mrs R Coleman And G Glendining

runners.name: Hostelry
runners.spotlight: Ten-time turf winner and her last success was at Carlisle (1m, good) last August; well held in last four starts but last two have been on AW and she may have needed her reappearance run last month; now 7lb lower than for her last win and she needs a close look back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Ten-time turf winner who is on a reduced mark and needs a close look back in this sphere

runners.name: Powerful Response
runners.spotlight: First win was on AW but last two have come on turf (8.3f/1m2f, soft); had some close calls on Tapeta during the winter and he was a creditable fourth in big field at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) 11 days ago; could be in the mix again but he wouldn't want the ground to dry out.
runners.comment: Dual turf winner who could be in the mix but he wouldn't want the ground to dry out

runners.name: Our Misty Blue
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who is far from solid on form and has yet to start at shorter than 33-1; cheekpieces are now added and he needs improvement on this switch to turf.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden who is far from solid on form; needs improvement on turf debut

runners.name: The Sweet Escape
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and no better than midfield on his last four starts; dropping down the weights but his best recent effort came in cheekpieces and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 0-12 and no better than midfield on his last four starts; others are more convincing

runners.name: Amber Honey
runners.spotlight: Hold-up filly who went close at Wolverhampton (8.6f) on her penultimate run but she didn't fire there last time; still lightly raced on turf but she's not easy to predict and needs to bounce back again after that last run; switch of headgear.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but she didn't fire at Wolverhampton last time; bit to prove back on turf

runners.name: Rogue Officer
runners.spotlight: A fair 7f/1m maiden for Peter Chapple-Hyam in 2025; changed hands for 7,000gns and he's finished ahead of only one rival in two runs for new yard this year; overall record is now 0-8 and he needs a big turnaround.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden and he's struggled in two runs for new yard this year; passed over

runners.name: Louie The Legend
runners.spotlight: Acts on good to firm and heavy ground; went close over C&D last October but he's struggled since including in three hurdle runs this year; on dangerous mark back on the Flat but he has a patchy profile and others are preferred.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark back on the Flat but he has a patchy profile and others are preferred

runners.name: Future Cutlet
runners.spotlight: Won in Ireland (7f) in 2024 but he was unplaced in six runs for new yard last year; mark continues to fall but he needs a transformation back up in trip after six months off.
runners.comment: Unplaced in six runs for current yard and he needs a transformation after 175 days off

runners.name: Token Love
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent seven-race maiden; raised his game with a close second in Wolverhampton handicap (9.4f) in December, but he faded on his return visit four weeks later; still has potential but he needs to step up again after 99 days off.
runners.comment: Inconsistent seven-race maiden and he needs to step up again after 99 days off

runners.name: Sir David
runners.spotlight: Came good with a last-gasp win at Carlisle (7f, good) last August but he ended last season with a heavy defeat on his final run for Stella Barclay; record is now 1-12 and he has something to prove on return for new yard.
runners.comment: Record of 1-12 and he ended last season with a heavy defeat; bit to prove for new yard

runners.name: Spirit Catcher
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner (7f/1m) but his last success was in August 2023 and he's been out of sorts since returning from a lengthy absence in September; mark is tumbling but he needs a major revival back on turf.
runners.comment: His last win was in 2023 and he's struggled in last four runs; lots to prove

races.name: Party Night 18th April Handicap

runners.name: Crimson Rambler
runners.spotlight: Exposed type but he has form figures of 222114 in AW handicaps this year and was beaten less than 2l when fourth at Wolverhampton (8.6f) last time; now returns to turf but he should get a good tow into this race and has possibilities if he can continue the good work.
runners.comment: Record of 222114 in AW handicaps; dangerous if he can continue the good work back to turf

runners.name: Aneirins Sword
runners.spotlight: Has done well in AW handicaps this year including two wins, four seconds and a third at Chelmsford (1m2f) two weeks ago; now makes her turf debut but she still has scope for further progress and yard won a division of this race with a similar type last year; in the mix.
runners.comment: Has done well in AW handicaps this year and she's respected on her turf debut

runners.name: Venetian Romance
runners.spotlight: Made a promising start when third at Lingfield (7f, good) last August; well held on AW in two subsequent runs for David Menuisier but she has plenty of stamina on dam's side and is a possible improver now upped up in trip on handicap/stable debut; needs watching in market with Rossa Ryan booked on her seasonal return.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly and she's a possible improver upped in trip on handicap/stable debut

runners.name: Smartanck
runners.spotlight: Raised his game after wind surgery when causing a 50-1 surprise in a Newcastle handicap (1m, AW) last month; that was a narrow success but he rallied well in the closing stages and is only 2lb higher here; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Improved form after wind surgery with a 50-1 win at Newcastle; 2lb higher back on turf

runners.name: Kokushoku
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in small-field events (7f/1m) in her first two runs this year and she had a tough assignment when down the field in useful novice at Southwell (1m, AW) last month; looks feasibly treated on her peak effort and this well-bred filly is an interesting contender on handicap debut; visor added.
runners.comment: Second in two of her qualifying runs and she needs close look on handicap debut; visor on

runners.name: Saint Of The Sea
runners.spotlight: Struggled in his sole 2yo run but he's shown clear promise with two placed efforts in novice events at Southwell (1m, AW) this year; looks on a fair opening mark and he's respected back on turf on his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Placed in two novice events at Southwell this year and he's respected on handicap debut

runners.name: Bearin Up
runners.spotlight: Improved for the step up to 1m when scoring at Bath (good) last September but she was never involved over C&D (fifth of nine) in final run last season; record is now 1-7 and she needs to kick on again after six months off.
runners.comment: Won at Bath last September but was well held over C&D in her final run last season

runners.name: Light The Night Up
runners.spotlight: Didn't really progress in four runs last season and was a fading third on her nursery debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) in November; still early days but she needs improvement back up in trip on her reappearance.
runners.comment: Still early days but she needs improvement back up in trip on her reappearance

runners.name: Bird Of War
runners.spotlight: 140,000gns yearling; made the breakthrough at the sixth attempt when surging 4l clear in a Newcastle handicap (1m, AW; 6-4 favourite) in November; 8lb higher on his return but he could have a lot more to offer as a 3yo and he's strongly respected back on turf.
runners.comment: Easy win at Newcastle in his final run last year; up 8lb but he's respected on return

runners.name: Gloriola
runners.spotlight: No impact in her three qualifying runs and she finished last of 14 on nursery debut at Leicester (6f, good to soft; 33-1) in October; still early days but has plenty to prove at this new trip on her return.
runners.comment: Finished last of 14 on nursery debut in October; plenty to prove at new trip on her return

runners.name: Del Corso
runners.spotlight: Has useful pedigree but he was well held in his three 2yo runs including over C&D (soft); gelded since his last run in December but he needs a transformation on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well held in three 2yo runs and he needs improvement on handicap debut; has been gelded

runners.name: Kokoluna
runners.spotlight: Close second in a Lingfield maiden (1m, AW) in December and things didn't go her way from wide draw at Wolverhampton later that month; Hollie Doyle is now booked on handicap debut and she could resume her progress back from a break; needs a close look in market.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of promise in a Lingfield maiden in December; in the mix on handicap debut

races.name: Watch Racing TV Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Jez Bomb
runners.spotlight: Had four wins in 2024 and another four last April-October; none of those were above Class 5 but he's been placed in some Class 4 events since November and things didn't go his way at Wolverhampton last time; should get a better tow into this race and he's in the mix back on turf.
runners.comment: Generally reliable type and things didn't go his way on Tapeta latest; dangerous

runners.name: Monty Beau
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo who won on handicap debut at Wolverhampton in December (8.6f) and has run to a similar level in three subsequent runs; needs to find more but he's unexposed at this trip and was beaten less than 1l when fifth at Newcastle (1m2f) last time; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 5yo who still has potential and is unexposed at this trip; not ruled out

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner including at this track; returned after six months off with a close call at Lingfield (1m2f) in February but he's been well held in both subsequent runs; now back on last winning mark but losing run is mounting up and he needs cheekpieces to give him a lift.
runners.comment: Course winner but losing run is mounting up and he needs cheekpieces to give him a lift

runners.name: Cavolo Nero
runners.spotlight: Turf (1m, good) and AW winner (7f) in 2025; ended last season with a placed effort at Lingfield and he reappeared with a good second at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) last month; looks interesting off unchanged mark and he has shaped as though this new trip should be within range.
runners.comment: Runner-up on Kempton reappearance and he could find more progress at this new trip

runners.name: Moon Beginnings
runners.spotlight: Started his career with a win at Chester (7f, good) and a second at Southwell (1m, AW) last autumn; looked a useful prospect in those two runs but he's finished a well-held eighth in his two handicaps since; still unexposed but he needs improvement at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 4yo but he needs improvement at this new trip
runners.owner: Nbr30 Mike Owen & Rasio Cymru Racing

runners.name: Just An Hour
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent gelding who won three times last season including on his reappearance over C&D (good; 3-1) last April; still looks on a workable mark and it wouldn't be a surprise if he made a bold bid after 166 days off.
runners.comment: Three wins last season including on his reappearance over C&D last April; needs close look

runners.name: Kind Of Kiss
runners.spotlight: 1m2f French winner; well handicapped on his best form for Kevin Philippart De Foy but he's struggled in last four starts including two runs for new yard this year; overall record is now 1-14 and he has something to prove.
runners.comment: Has struggled in his last four runs and overall record is now 1-14; down the list

runners.name: Secret Beach
runners.spotlight: Struggled on hurdling/stable at Newbury in December but his last Flat run resulted in a brave win at Newmarket (1m4f, good) in the autumn; still looks feasibly treated on that form and he's in the mix back from another break; had a second wind operation since his last run.
runners.comment: Struggled on hurdling/stable debut in December but his last Flat run resulted in a win

runners.name: Mighty Quiet
runners.spotlight: Has patchy profile but she scored at Windsor (1m2f, good) last September and ended last season with a good second in the mud at Bath; returns after 160 days off but her first win was after a break and she's only 3lb higher than for her last success; one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Has patchy record but she's a dual winner who needs a close look on her reappearance

runners.name: Love Beach
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden at Haydock (1m, good) last May but he's not really gone on from that in handicaps since; ended last season with two respectable thirds at this track (8.3f/1m2f) but he needs to find something extra after 161 days off.
runners.comment: Still has low mileage but he needs to find more after 161 days off

races.name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Apprentice Handicap

runners.name: Tommytwohoots
runners.spotlight: Finished second in this race in 2023 (soft) and 2024 (heavy); good second at Wolverhampton on his stable debut (6f) two weeks ago; well drawn back on turf but his 18 turf runs have seen him steer clear of anything faster than good to soft.
runners.comment: Twice second in the corresponding race; unraced on faster than good to soft

runners.name: Doralee
runners.spotlight: Gained her only win from 16 attempts in a 5f classified event at Redcar last August; shaped as though the run was needed on her AW return last month; others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: Only win came in classified company; sharper for last month's return; others appeal more

runners.name: Sir Benedict
runners.spotlight: The winner of seven of his 80 races; respectable second on his first run for Ben Haslam four weeks ago (5f, Tapeta); drawn wide and much will depend on how much traffic he encounters.
runners.comment: Needs everything to drop right and his wide stall could pose a problem

runners.name: Golden Prosperity
runners.spotlight: Three of his four wins have come in classified company but the other was a C&D handicap; this mark in range judged on last season's best efforts and he is high on the list from a handy draw.
runners.comment: C&D winner; returns to turf off a fair mark; well drawn and he is one to take seriously

runners.name: Baby Rover
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f handicap at Redcar last summer; runner-up twice afterwards but she has been absent since a low-key run at Newcastle 190 days ago; best form has come at 6f.
runners.comment: Her one win and most of her best runs have come over 6f; best watched after 190 days off
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Beneficiary
runners.spotlight: Three times a runner-up on AW this year; unplaced in five turf runs and he comes here with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Good chance if converting AW form to turf but hasn't managed that in five attempts before

runners.name: Latin Five
runners.spotlight: Has run well over C&D, including when a close third in this race 12 months ago, but the track doesn't always lend itself to his hold-up style; drawn widest and could prove vulnerable, for all he is attractively weighted.
runners.comment: On a good mark; close third in this race last year; may prove vulnerable from a wide stall

runners.name: Invitorio
runners.spotlight: Yet to win a handicap but he was twice placed off a 7lb higher mark last September; two AW runs last month should have sharpened him up and this is a drop in grade; apprentice takes off a valuable 5lb but a wide draw on his first attempt at 5f, and a sharp one at that, dents confidence.
runners.comment: Likely to win races off his reduced mark but wide draw and drop in trip both concerns
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing Xviii And Ruth Carr

runners.name: Running Cool
runners.spotlight: Good second at Wolverhampton on his stable debut last September; well short of that level in three runs this year, including when tried in cheekpieces 19 days ago; risky.
runners.comment: Should be well handicapped but he has been disappointing so far in 2026

runners.name: Siviez
runners.spotlight: Five runs, all over further, and she has run to just a modest level; drops to 5f with a point to prove.
runners.comment: Poor form over 7f and 1m; drops in trip with a point to prove

runners.name: Jamies Choice
runners.spotlight: Won two classified events over 5f on turf in 2024; she finished a close second in the corresponding race 12 months ago (after an identical 204-day absence) but that was her only bright spot last year; 7lb lower this time around and she can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Struggled after finishing second in this race in 2025; 7lb lower this time around

runners.name: Soul Seeker
runners.spotlight: Regressed sharply in 2025, his first blank campaign since 2020; he has dropped a long way in the weights as a result and he now tackles his first 0-55 on grass; whether first time out is the time to catch him is a moot point too.
runners.comment: Had poor year in 2025; down in weights as a result but no great record after an absence

races.name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: Contorno
runners.spotlight: Justified strong market support on his 5f Newcastle handicap debut in January; finished behind the reopposing Liverpool Star at Southwell next time but resumed his progression when landing another 5f Newcastle handicap last month; edged out a next-time-out winner on that occasion so should still be feasibly treated after a 2lb nudge.
runners.comment: 2-3 in AW handicaps; edged out a next-time-out winner latest so 2lb nudge may not stop him

runners.name: Regal Dream
runners.spotlight: Could only manage fourth of eight on her 5f Redcar handicap debut (good) in October but the fact she went off the 13-8 favourite suggests better was anticipated; remains unexposed and it'll be interesting to see if there's money for her again back from six months off.
runners.comment: Only fourth on nursery debut in October but was 13-8 favourite; first run since

runners.name: O Fortuna
runners.spotlight: No more than hinted at ability in three outings last year but this half-brother to the smart sprinter Adresita rates a potential improver now moving into handicap company for a yard which has won the last two runnings of this race; has been gelded; interesting, particularly if backed.
runners.comment: Potential improver in handicaps for yard with good recent record in this; interesting

runners.name: Liverpool Star
runners.spotlight: Edged out by Brave Traveller at Newcastle (6f) off this mark in December and respectable efforts in three subsequent AW outings; should be competitive back on turf.
runners.comment: In fair form on AW ahead of this return to turf; ought to be thereabouts

runners.name: Brave Traveller
runners.spotlight: Edged out Liverpool Star to win a 6f Newcastle handicap in December; faded into fifth on his reappearance there in March but is entitled to strip fitter for the outing; showed promise on turf at the start of his career; respected.
runners.comment: 6f Newcastle win in December; possibly just needed recent reappearance there; respected

runners.name: Rotokura Belle
runners.spotlight: Cosy winner over 5f at Southwell last September but hasn't come come close to reproducing that in three subsequent AW starts; it remains to be seen whether a return to turf sparks a revival.
runners.comment: Cosy winner at Southwell last autumn but below that level in three subsequent AW starts
runners.owner: Crazy Racing Ltd & Miss D Francis

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: Best effort for George Scott when beaten a neck in Wolverhampton handicap (5f, AW) in October but hasn't come close to backing it up in three outings since, including yard debut 34 days ago; plenty to prove for now.
runners.comment: Close second at Wolverhampton in October but well beaten since, including yard debut

runners.name: Empress Olivia
runners.spotlight: Won Ripon maiden (6f, good) last summer; respectable efforts in her first two handicaps (both 6f turf) but well held over 7f on AW final start; drops back to the minimum trip on her return from five months off; others are more obvious.
runners.comment: 6f turf maiden winner but fourth is the best she managed in three handicaps last year

runners.name: Jack Rabbit Slims
runners.spotlight: Showed the odd bit of promise last year but finished down the field in his two handicaps; back from six months off with his yard in reasonable form.
runners.comment: Odd bit of promise last year but well held in both handicaps; been off for 196 days

runners.name: Dabbling
runners.spotlight: On the back of quite promising efforts on her first three starts she was backed into 2-1 favouritism for her Carlisle handicap debut in September but finished last of nine and was subsequently found to have bled; off since; it'll be interesting to see whether her supporters are prepared to give her another chance.
runners.comment: 2-1 for handicap debut but finished last of nine and was found to have bled; off since

races.name: Visit racingtv.com Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Jeddaal
runners.spotlight: Fair form at around 7f on going ranging from good to firm to good to soft last year; below par when turned over at short odds over 6f on soft final start; reappears having been gelded; sure to be thereabouts if recapturing anywhere near the best of his 2yo form.
runners.comment: Fair form over 7f last year and likely to be on the premises again on reappearance

runners.name: Red Spells Danger
runners.spotlight: Promise in defeat on all four 7f starts last year; the form of his penultimate third in a soft-ground novice at Ayr has worked out really well and he's capable of winning races in 2026.
runners.comment: Promise on all four 7f starts as a juvenile and capable of winning races in 2026

runners.name: North Force
runners.spotlight: Frankel colt out of a Group 2 winner; promise when placed over 6f at Doncaster (good) and 7f at Redcar (good to soft) last year; shaped particularly well at the latter venue given he lost plenty of ground at the start when his blindfold appeared to get stuck; definitely more to come.
runners.comment: Well-bred colt who shaped well when placed over 6f/7f last year; definitely more to come

runners.name: Decem Starr
runners.spotlight: Left debut effort behind to finish second at Leicester (7f, good) last September; respectable fifth in another 7f novice at Redcar (good to soft) later in the autumn; gelded ahead of reappearance; ought to be thereabouts.
runners.comment: Fair form at 2, notably 7f Leicester second; gelded ahead of return; thereabouts

runners.name: Forest Phoenix
runners.spotlight: 50,000gns gelded son of Lope De Vega and the second foal of a 1m Listed winner; bred to have a future but Red Spells Danger has to be considered the stable's first string unless the betting strongly hints otherwise.
runners.comment: Bred to have future but Red Spells Danger yard first string unless betting says otherwise

runners.name: Talking In Kode
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners Belacqua (7f/1m AW 2yo; RPR 90), Notimeforchitchat (7f/1m AW; 86) and Sullivan Bay (13.3f/2m AW; 65); dam 7f AW winner only start; from a respected yard and would enter calculations if the betting speaks in her favour.
runners.comment: From a respected yard and would enter calculations if the betting speaks in her favour

runners.name: Keep It Classic
runners.spotlight: Useful pedigree but finished down the field in three 7f outings towards the end of 2025; gelded ahead of reappearance; will likely have bigger performances in him this year but possibly not until handicapping.
runners.comment: Type to do better for leading yard but possibly not until handicapping

runners.name: The Floors Munky
runners.spotlight: Well held on both starts seven months apart (trained by Iain Jardine on debut); likely outsider.
runners.comment: Well held on both starts and a likely outsider

runners.name: Hatamoto
runners.spotlight: More than hinted at ability when fifth in two 6f outings on good last summer but he might be another in this line-up who won't really come into his own until handicapping; gelded since last seen.
runners.comment: Some promise in two 6f starts last summer but handicaps may be more his thing

runners.name: Finlaggan
runners.spotlight: French recruit who didn't offer immediate promise for shrewd new connections at Southwell (1m; 16-1) 13 days ago.
runners.comment: No short-term promise for shrewd new connections on AW last month

runners.name: Hansteen
runners.spotlight: Well held in two outings in the mud last autumn and likely to be more one for handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Well held in two outings in the mud last autumn and likely more one for handicaps

races.name: Bet At Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Farandaway
runners.spotlight: Running well in defeat in 5f/6f handicaps on AW prior to getting off the mark at the 17th attempt in Newcastle maiden (6f, AW) three weeks ago; positive performances on all three previous visits here; untested at 7f but it's possible the trip will be within range; in the mix if seeing it out.
runners.comment: In good form over 5f/6f on AW and in the mix if seeing out this new trip

runners.name: Evocative Spark
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Southwell (1m, AW) in February on sole run this year but he's just 1-22 on AW as opposed to 5-31 on turf; won in good style over C&D (soft) last October and, while slow ground would boost confidence in his claims, he may be okay on good going; on the shortlist now back here.
runners.comment: Won in good style over C&D last October and he's on the shortlist now back here

runners.name: Hostility
runners.spotlight: 13-race maiden who wasn't at his best towards the end of last year; however, he's dropped to a dangerous mark and it will be no surprise if he's in the shake-up on this reappearance, for which he wears a first-time visor.
runners.comment: Below best towards end of last year and 0-13, but he's dropped to a dangerous mark

runners.name: Yafaarr
runners.spotlight: In fine form for new yard last autumn, predominantly on AW but also when close third at Redcar (7f, good); he can play a leading role if continuing the good work on this return to action.
runners.comment: In fine form for new yard last autumn; leading player if continuing good work on return

runners.name: Tarlac
runners.spotlight: He's 6lb lower than when winning at Ripon (6f, good to soft) last May and he may have needed his recent comeback run at Newcastle; however, he was also below his best on his final few starts last term and 6f may suit best.
runners.comment: 6lb below last May's last winning mark but there was no return to form on recent comeback
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Precious Spartan
runners.spotlight: Progressive after joining this yard last May, winning three times at Ripon (1m, good) and posting a close third at Ayr (1m, soft) in September when last seen; the drop back to 7f might not be ideal on his reappearance but he's one to consider nevertheless.
runners.comment: Progressive after joining this yard last May and he's one to consider on his reappearance

runners.name: Coconut Bay
runners.spotlight: She lost her form last autumn but won four times during a busy 2025, two of them over C&D (good/good to firm), and a break may have helped; she's 3lb below her last winning mark and could have a big say.
runners.comment: She lost her form last autumn but a break may have helped; two C&D wins last summer

runners.name: Roundhay Park
runners.spotlight: Course winner who scored at Ripon (6f, soft) last July and is just 2lb higher on this reappearance outing; however, 6f has been this 11yo's optimum trip in recent seasons, and this is his first run since October.
runners.comment: Won at Ripon last July when 2lb lower; 6f seems to be this 11yo's optimum trip nowadays

runners.name: Highfield Sunshine
runners.spotlight: Below best at the end of last season and she's 0-12; wears a first-time hood for this return, having worn blinkers then cheekpieces on final two runs last term; on a handy mark on last season's best efforts (including over C&D) and she's not discounted.
runners.comment: 12-race maiden but on a handy mark on last season's best form, including over C&D

runners.name: Wadacre Maestro
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent last year but won at Musselburgh (7f, good) in August on his third handicap start and wasn't beaten far in fifth at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) when last seen; remains lightly raced and could be a contender.
runners.comment: Inconsistent last year but won in August and remains lightly raced; could be a contender

runners.name: Dr Rio
runners.spotlight: All six of his wins in Britain, and all ten of his outings last season, have come at Beverley; won over 7.4f/1m4f last summer but this 10yo lacks a recent outing and hasn't really shone on his three previous visits here.
runners.comment: Beverley specialist; now ten and lacks a recent run; hasn't shone here previously

runners.name: Way To Dubai
runners.spotlight: He's won just once since making his British debut in October 2023; however, that came this January (7f, AW) and a slow start proved costly when back at 6f at Southwell last Thursday; effective on turf and might not be far away now back up in trip.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate in Britain but has managed a win this year and might not be far away

runners.name: Crocodile Power
runners.spotlight: His last four wins have come over 6f here; can't be ruled out given his record here and one of those wins came on good ground, but soft going appears to bring out the best in him and 6f looks to be his optimum trip; races for the first-time since December.
runners.comment: His last four wins have come here, but over 6f, and soft ground appears to be preferable

runners.name: Vince Le Prince
runners.spotlight: 0-16 last year but runner-up four times, including over C&D last October when last seen; he's run well on his reappearance in each of the last two seasons and this three-time C&D winner could be bang there.
runners.comment: 0-16 last year but runner-up four times and he goes well fresh; could be bang there

races.name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Free Pic
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) last October and close fourth there (1m4f, AW) in February; didn't see out 2m there last month; also effective on turf and she's one to consider now back down in trip.
runners.comment: 1m4f AW winner; didn't see out 2m last month; also effective on turf; one to consider

runners.name: Wheres The Crumpet
runners.spotlight: Now 1lb lower than when winning over 1m at Carlisle last May, and there have been a couple of good runs over 1m on AW since; however, she was below par last month and it's a long time since she ran over a trip this far; arrives with something to prove.
runners.comment: Won off 1lb higher over 1m at Carlisle last May; a long time since she ran over this far

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: Without a win since June 2024 (1m6f, good to soft) and inconsistent last year, but he finished placed on a few occasions; reappears off a career-low mark and he's not discounted.
runners.comment: Winless last year and inconsistent, but placed a few times & reappears off career-low mark

runners.name: Tupero
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) in February when dropped back in trip on his second handicap start; not devoid of promise in France last May on sole run on turf; now things have clicked, this lightly raced and well-bred 4yo is in excellent hands to continue to progress; very much respected up 4lb.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle on second handicap start; in excellent hands to continue to progress

runners.name: V Twelve
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over hurdles in October but he won off today's mark at Hamilton (1m5f, good) last summer and can go well fresh; a possible.
runners.comment: Returns from a break and won off today's mark last June; can go well fresh; a possible

runners.name: Stellarmasterpiece
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins last year (good/good to firm), including this race; ran okay when fifth of 13 over hurdles here in January last time and she's a key player if at the top of her game.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins last year, including this race; key player if at the top of her game

runners.name: Its Only Fun
runners.spotlight: Won at Musselburgh (1m1f, good) last April and took step back in right direction when sixth of 14 at Doncaster (1m2f, good to soft) recently; yet to prove his stamina for 1m4f but could be in the mix if seeing it out.
runners.comment: Step back in right direction when sixth of 14 over 1m2f at Doncaster; unproven at 1m4f

runners.name: Nakatomi
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when sixth of ten at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) two starts ago but well beaten since and now 0-16; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly when sixth of ten two starts ago but well beaten since and now 0-16
runners.owner: F Reay S McMillan & S Pedersen

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: 0-27 for previous yard and down the field at 40-1 on stable debut at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) three weeks ago; he's hard to fancy.
runners.comment: 0-27 for previous yard and down the field at 40-1 on stable debut at Newcastle last month

runners.name: Ebony Maw
runners.spotlight: Course winner who is on a competitive mark on last season's efforts, which came over 1m5f-2m1f; however, this 9yo went 0-11 last term and today's reappearance trip may prove to be inadequate.
runners.comment: On a competitive mark on last year's best but went 0-11 and he may prefer further

races.name: Every Race Live On Racing TV Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Eagles Whistle
runners.spotlight: Won three classified events at up to 9.4f in the space of four days in January when trained by Daragh Bourke; better effort in handicaps for new yard when second off 11 at Wolverhampton (8.6f) 19 days ago; good shout on that form buts need to prove that he's as effective on turf (0-9) and that he has the stamina for this much longer trip.
runners.comment: In good form on AW at up to 9.4f this year but much longer trip an unknown back on turf

runners.name: Hickton
runners.spotlight: In good form last autumn, including 1m4f wins Hamilton (soft) and Southwell (AW); the fact he bled when well beaten on his reappearance back at Southwell is a concern but he's a major player on his 2025 form.
runners.comment: Bled on last month's reappearance but major player if back to his 2025 form.

runners.name: Roland Garros
