runners.spotlight: Maiden who returned from wind surgery to finish a respectable fifth on AW Flat and backed that up when 15l second in a 2m maiden hurdle at Southwell (soft) last month; a lesser effort in a handicap hurdle at Wetherby three weeks ago needs forgiving but he dips into a Class 6 for the first time now back on the Flat and has the ability to make his mark at this level; cheekpieces rejoin the tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Maiden under both codes but makes some appeal now dropping into his first Class 6

runners.name: Gastronomy
runners.spotlight: Out of sorts when last seen in the autumn but is well treated if a subsequent breathing operation has worked, having won off 8lb higher on turf in summer 2024; worth a betting check with Phil Kirby among the winners.
runners.comment: Well treated if a breathing operation since last seen has worked; needs a betting check

runners.name: Rock Armour
runners.spotlight: Expensive to follow for John Butler, aside from a 1m2f Newcastle win last February, and has struggled in six runs for this yard; remains to be seen whether a return to turf improves matters.
runners.comment: Has struggled in six AW runs for this yard and remains to be seen if return to turf helps

runners.name: Muhib
runners.spotlight: 0-18 but the fact he arrives on the back of creditable in-frame efforts over 1m4f at Newcastle and Wolverhampton in recent months makes him of some interest in race where most have something to prove.
runners.comment: 0-18 but does arrive in form which is more than can be said for most of these

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: Regressive maiden who trailed throughout on her return from a break at Newcastle in February.
runners.comment: Regressive maiden who trailed throughout on return from a break at Newcastle in February

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: 1m5f winner at Ayr (good) off this mark last summer; ended 2025 out of sorts on AW but feasibly handicapped if returning to form back on turf after five months off.
runners.comment: Out of sorts on AW when last seen but did win off this mark on turf last summer

runners.name: Stitching Wheel
runners.spotlight: Displayed early promise on the Flat in France but hasn't shown much since coming to Britain, including over hurdles; first outing for 138 days.
runners.comment: Early promise on Flat in France but hasn't shown much in Britain, including hurdles

races.name: racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Progressive in 2024, including a C&D win, and he made a bright start to 2025 too; a wind op last June helped turn things around in the autumn; blew the start at Newcastle on last month's reappearance; capable of a big run, for all he would be more appealing on a slower surface.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fluffed the start on his return but he is capable off this mark; considered

runners.name: Secret Guest
runners.spotlight: Mixed bag this winter but he returns to turf on a handy mark; this trip probably his best and a revival could be forthcoming.
runners.comment: In and out this winter; returns to turf/optimum trip off a dangerous mark; could revive

runners.name: Aberama Gold
runners.spotlight: Won three times in 2025, each of them off today's mark or higher; conceding age to his rivals but it would be no surprise to see him give a good account.
runners.comment: Three wins last year; well treated for his return to turf; should give a good account

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 in top form, following a 5f Pontefract win with a C&D success eight days later (both on soft ground); has gone well fresh in the past; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 with two soft-ground wins, including C&D; has gone well fresh; not ruled out

runners.name: Valley Ofthe Kings
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins last year; didn't run badly in a stronger handicap at Doncaster ten days ago (not well drawn as things panned out); each-way shout going back around a left-hand bend.
runners.comment: Fair effort in a better race at Doncaster ten days ago; both wins on AW; each-way shout

runners.name: Sir Garfield
runners.spotlight: Three wins last year included one at Catterick (7f) and one off this mark (6f, good); absent five months and he will need to be at his best to make a winning return.
runners.comment: Course winner; returns on a winning mark but he'll need to be at his best in this field

runners.name: Miraculous
runners.spotlight: His two highest RPRs for Roger Varian came around a left-hand bend at Chester, winning off 1lb lower last July; ended 2025 with two lesser efforts, after which he was sold for 28,000gns; well drawn for his stable debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: Acts well around a left-hand bend; feasible mark for stable debut; betting can guide

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who won five of his first eight starts for Ruth Carr in the first half of 2025; ran well in defeat in the second half of the year too, placed in the Ayr Bronze Cup off 1lb higher; six-month absence to overcome but he shouldn't be underestimated.
runners.comment: Had fine 2025 and he returns to action off a manageable mark; shouldn't be underestimated

runners.name: Franciscos Piece
runners.spotlight: Smart 2yo; struggled in good handicaps last year but he has slipped down the weights and begins this campaign in a lower grade; one to keep a close eye on this year but drying ground isn't in his favour (best on soft/heavy).
runners.comment: Starts 2026 off a reduced mark; interesting sprinter but drying ground not in his favour

races.name: Racing Again 22nd April Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Four handicap wins last year, including at this track (7f, good); her recent return to action at Newcastle should have sharpened her up; not discounted.
runners.comment: Four handicap wins last year, including here (7f, good); sharper for recent return; chance

runners.name: Moostar
runners.spotlight: Back to form with a tongue-tie fitted on her last two starts, second at Chelmsford in December and third at Wolverhampton over Christmas (both 6f); still on a good mark and she is a strong contender from her low draw.
runners.comment: Back to form in a tongue-tie on her last two starts; returns to turf on a competitive mark

runners.name: Fillys Last Lady
runners.spotlight: May come on for last month's seasonal return (5f, AW) and her two wins have been on turf; better expected this time.
runners.comment: Return to turf will suit & she should be sharper for last month's return; better expected

runners.name: Paroda Diva
runners.spotlight: Trained by Stan Moore as a 2yo; won a 7f fillies' maiden (good) last May and ran well at Kempton on her nursery debut in July (7f); ended her 2yo campaign quietly and she starts out for a new yard (same owners) with a point to prove.
runners.comment: 7f turf win as a 2yo; ended 2025 quietly; makes stable debut with a point to prove

runners.name: Fizzy Cristal
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with two good runs in Class 6 AW nurseries, second and first at Newcastle in October; today's 3lb higher mark probably not beyond her; respected.
runners.comment: Ended her 2yo campaign on the up; raised in class but not fully exposed

runners.name: Keldeo
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins here in 2024; satisfactory return to action at Southwell last month but today's sixth furlong could leave her vulnerable.
runners.comment: Two course wins over 5f; satisfactory return last month but the sixth furlong is a worry
runners.owner: Ownaracehorse O Pears and T&T Racing Ltd

runners.name: Indys Angel
runners.spotlight: Failed to win in 2025; conditions won't be an excuse but she returns from a 166-day break and is 2lb out of the weights; others look safer.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem but she drew a blank in 2025 and runs from out of the weights today

runners.name: One Of Our Own
runners.spotlight: All wins in Class 6 company, three of them coming over C&D in 2024; she was running well when last seen but her record fresh doesn't suggest this will be her day.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; ended 2025 in good form but her record fresh is offputting

races.name: Mercedes-Benz Of Chichester Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cluain Chormaic
runners.spotlight: Third in big-field maiden at Leopardstown's Christmas festival (2m4f, good to yielding) and again ran well when second at Huntingdon (2m, good) for new stable last month; back up in trip here and has another big role to play.
runners.comment: Placed in big-field maidens on last two starts (2m4f/2m) and has another big role to play

runners.name: Hardly Surprising
runners.spotlight: Point winner (yielding ground) who was placed on the last two of his three hurdling starts, more recently when third of 13 at Doncaster (2m3f, soft) in January; will be right in the thick of things if handling the drying ground.
runners.comment: Good third a Doncaster in January; big player if handling the quicker ground

runners.name: Jetaway Holiday
runners.spotlight: Joined Nicky Henderson after winning a fast-ground point last spring; made low-key rules debut when well-held fifth in Warwick bumper (good to soft) last month but ought to be capable of better.
runners.comment: Point winner; made low-key rules debut in a bumper last month but is open to improvement

runners.name: Testing Patience
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when last of four finishers at Kempton (2m5f, good) on last month's hurdling debut but might have been flattered by his proximity there and others here have much more convincing form.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when last of four finishers on hurdling debut but others have stronger form

runners.name: Graeme The Grey
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise in his bumpers but was well held when fourth here (2m1f, good to soft) on recent hurdling debut and has a lot to find with the principals.
runners.comment: Showed some promise in bumpers but was well held when fourth here on hurdling debut

runners.name: Mega Etoile
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging bumper debut on heavy ground in February but last month's run on good ground was a backward step; can only be watched on hurdling debut.
runners.comment: First bumper run was okay but the second was a backward step; hurdle debut today

runners.name: Cockney Cleopatra
runners.spotlight: Ran well when fifth of nine behind short-odds winner at Clonmel (2m, soft) in November, her third hurdling start, but this stable debut comes after a five-month absence and she needs to improve again.
runners.comment: Ran well on final start in Ireland but needs to improve again on this stable debut

runners.name: Snowski
runners.spotlight: Struggled when a big-priced outsider for 2m4f Plumpton maiden last month; now sports cheekpieces; stable has much more obvious chance with Cluain Chormaic.
runners.comment: Struggled when a big-priced outsider for 2m4f Plumpton maiden last month

runners.name: Maximum Benefit
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced in two slow-ground hurdles this year (2m/2m1f) and could plausibly be seen to better effect on this quicker surface but low-grade handicaps are probably next on his agenda.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in two slow-ground hurdles this year but low-grade handicaps beckon

runners.name: Knight Templar
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in Irish points last season but was safely held in two soft-ground maiden hurdles this year (2m/2m1f; raced too freely), and was withdrawn from a race in between with good ground deemed unsuitable.
runners.comment: Showed promise in Irish points last season but safely held in two maiden hurdles this year

runners.name: Style De Folie
runners.spotlight: With a good stable and has a good pedigree but managed only fifth of six on bumper debut here (good to soft) on Boxing Day.
runners.comment: With a good stable but managed only fifth of six in a bumper here on debut

runners.name: Porridge
runners.spotlight: Pulled up all four starts this season, twice off lowly marks in handicaps, and makes no appeal.
runners.comment: Pulled up all four starts this season, twice off lowly marks in handicaps; makes no appeal

races.name: Last Resort Club Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Mammies Boy
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner (2m3f-2m5f, good) for the O'Neill stable but well beaten on both outings for new trainer this year; wore cheekpieces last time; blinkers given a whirl here.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both starts for new stable this year; blinkers given a whirl today

runners.name: The Secret Pearl
runners.spotlight: Absent since slightly disappointing run at Aintree on Boxing Day but returned from break with two good seconds over 2m4f in the autumn (good/soft) and has good claims if back at that level here.
runners.comment: Off since below-par run on Boxing Day but her autumn form gives her good claims

runners.name: Duffry Trixs
runners.spotlight: Battled well to win Taunton maiden (2m3f, good to soft) last season but has not been in the same form in any of his four handicaps this term (2m4f-3m); drops back in trip here; needs to up his game.
runners.comment: Battled well for maiden win last season; yet to match that form in handicaps this term

runners.name: Northanger Abbey
runners.spotlight: 0-4 over hurdles but made quite promising handicap debut when sixth of 13 at Warwick (2m, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) last month and this move back up in trip may enable improvement; one to consider.
runners.comment: Made quite promising handicap debut over 2m last month; moves back up in trip here

runners.name: Tigga Time
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at seventh attempt, at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in November; disappointed on only appearance this year (in February) but current mark ought to be workable if he's back on song.
runners.comment: Disappointed on only appearance this year but this mark is workable if he's back on song

runners.name: Herbygoesbananas
runners.spotlight: Ready bumper winner in 2024; showed only minor promise in first three hurdle races, with best effort probably his sixth over C&D (soft) on second occasion, but is open to improvement on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; only minor promise over hurdles so far; may improve in handicaps

runners.name: Galactic Charm
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of last season's form but looked unenthusiastic when never-dangerous fourth at Plumpton (2m4f, good to soft) last month and has become disappointing; needs change of headgear to perk him up.
runners.comment: Looked unenthusiastic when never-dangerous fourth last time; headgear switched here

runners.name: Lyle View
runners.spotlight: Displayed significant improvement to win in good style at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft; well backed) in February 2025, her sixth start over hurdles; could quite plausibly have more to offer but this is her first run since; market instructive.
runners.comment: Won in good style when last in action but that was in February 2025; market instructive

races.name: Southern Cranes And Access Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Duhallow Tommy
runners.spotlight: Four chase wins, the latest from the front off reduced mark at Southwell (2m4f, good to soft) in February; just a respectable third of five at Wincanton (2m4f, good to soft) last month but could be hard to catch if able to dominate here.
runners.comment: Four chase wins, the latest off reduced mark in February; a possible if able to dominate

runners.name: Hawaii Du Mestivel
runners.spotlight: Progressive over hurdles in 2024, winning three times, but missed 2025 and has been pulled up in two chases this year; combination of return to better ground and first-time cheekieces might aid a revival but others in this field have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Progressive over hurdles in 2024; missed 2025; pulled up in two chases this year

runners.name: Rip Wheeler
runners.spotlight: Quirky customer who doesn't find much when striking the front but has won three times under well-timed Robert Dunne rides this season; unraced beyond about 2m4f under rules but well suited by good/good to soft ground and probably still on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Quirky customer who has won three times under well-timed Robert Dunne rides this season

runners.name: Maclaine
runners.spotlight: Now only 1lb higher than for emphatic win at Newbury (2m6f, good to soft) in March 2024 but absent since a lesser effort the following October and entitled to benefit from this outing.
runners.comment: Absent since below-par run in October 2024 and entitled to benefit from this outing

races.name: Land & Power Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ferret Jeeter
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner this season and added to his tally on last month's chasing debut at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft), asserting on run-in to score by 5l; only 3lb higher for this return to hurdling and won't mind if the ground ends up good; sports first-time blinkers; must be considered.
runners.comment: Three hurdles wins this season; back in this sphere after winning chase debut last month

runners.name: Staff Sergeant Len
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Syd Hosie with disappointing chase run in January but arguably posted career-best performance when close third of 14 over hurdles at Newbury (3m, good to soft) for new stable last month; quicker ground will also suit; respected.
runners.comment: Arguably showed career-best form when placed on recent stable debut a Newbury

runners.name: Confinentic
runners.spotlight: Yet to hit top gear after four runs this season and was behind Ferret Jeeter when remote third at Plumpton (3m1f, good to soft) last month but his fifth of 18 at Ascot (2m7f, good to soft) two starts ago came in a much stronger race than this; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear this season but his Ascot run two starts ago makes him of interest

runners.name: Vendant
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but returned from 11-month break with fairly good second here (2m5f, good to soft; no match for winner) 12 days ago and could plausibly still have untapped potential over this longer trip.
runners.comment: 0-8 over hurdles but came second here (2m5f) recently, after a layoff

runners.name: Godot
runners.spotlight: Didn't run badly when fifth of seven here (2m5f, good to soft; usual headgear removed) 12 days ago and has become well handicapped but this season has been largely disappointing and he's not yet proven over this far.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly here recently but this season has largely been disappointing
runners.owner: A Lewers D Shaw A Irvine

races.name: Southern Contract Lifting Handicap Chase

runners.name: Wellwillya
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner this season (2m1f/2m5f); ran big race in defeat at Exeter (2m3f, good to soft) last month and is also well suited by this track; ought to make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner this season; ran big race in defeat last month and this track suits well

runners.name: Valirann Gold
runners.spotlight: Produced remarkable late surge to snatch what had looked an unlikely victory here (2m5f, good to soft; blinkers removed) 12 days ago; recent 3lb rise is manageable but this inconsistent 9yo tends to race lazily nowadays and it's hard to predict his mood today.
runners.comment: Produced remarkable late surge to win here recently but is too inconsistent to rely upon

runners.name: Rap Soul
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles in the autumn but 2-2 since switched to handicap chases this year, with both wins gained in small fields at Plumpton (2m4f, soft/good); has a bit more on his plate today but may still have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: 2-2 since switched to chasing this year and could easily have more improvement to come

runners.name: Gorcombe Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Just 1-33 under rules but the win came here (in 2023) and he gave a good account when second from out of the weights at Hereford (2m5f, good) last month, after a break; could have a say.
runners.comment: Has modest strike-rate but gave good account last month, after a break

runners.name: Millies Mite
runners.spotlight: Gained both wins over C&D on good ground, including 2024 edition of this race; absent since pulled up on consecutive outings towards end of last year but it won't be a shock if she bounces back to form; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Ended 2025 in poor form but both her wins were gained over C&D; revival possible

runners.name: Misteroddsocks
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden with unconvincing record over fences this season (five attempts; remote when third last time); needs recent wind op to have made a difference.
runners.comment: Has unconvincing record over fences this season; needs recent wind op to make a difference

runners.name: Engaging Sam
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in all three hurdle races this season and now 0-12 in that sphere; swaps usual cheekpieces for first-time visor on this chasing debut; 4lb out of the weights; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well beaten in all three hurdle races this season and 4lb wrong for this chasing debut

runners.name: Kill The Groove
runners.spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles but made the frame on all three outings this year (all over 2m at Plumpton); moves back up in trip for this chasing debut with cheekpieces (worn once) removed; seems versatile regarding ground; can feature if he gets into a good jumping rhythm.
runners.comment: Made the frame on all three hurdling starts this year and has possibilities on chase debut
runners.owner: D Hill N Emanuel & B Chantler

races.name: KSR Lighting, The Ultimate Lighting Solutions Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Frank Stamper
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite when only seventh at Wincanton (1m7f, good to soft) last month and now 0-5 over hurdles but stayed on from poor position for second at Plumpton (2m, soft) two starts ago and may still have potential.
runners.comment: Beaten favourite last time but has claims if judged on earlier Plumpton second

runners.name: Ikigai Star
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdle winner on good ground; arguably ran better than eventual 48l defeat suggests when third at Huntingdon (2m3f, good) last month, after a layoff, and has become well handicapped; could have a say if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly last month, after a break, and has become well handicapped

runners.name: Ballylicky Bay
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner; soundly beaten on first two hurdling starts but her 200-1 fifth at Warwick (2m, good to soft) last month was much better and she's open to further improvement in handicaps.
runners.comment: Improved form when 200-1 fifth at Warwick last month; now makes handicap debut

runners.name: Tommy Dillion
runners.spotlight: Put in pretty good front-running shift when third over C&D (good to soft) 12 days ago but he's a lot more exposed than some of these, and his two hurdling wins were gained on heavy ground.
runners.comment: Ran well over C&D recently but the drying ground is not in his favour today

runners.name: Master Dunraven
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer; flopped when odds-on at Wetherby six weeks ago and is now 0-8 over hurdles but suggested his turn might be near when keeping-on third at Plumpton (2m, soft) two starts ago, and it will be interesting to see whether his supporters wade in again here.
runners.comment: Flopped when odds-on last time and now 0-8 over hurdles but major player on January form

runners.name: Jacquetta
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on all three hurdling starts (2m1f-2m3f) but has a lowly mark for this handicap debut and good market support will bring him into the equation; sports first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Has lowly mark for this handicap debut and good market support will make him of interest

races.name: Virgin Bet/EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Littlecote
runners.spotlight: Improved from last autumn's debut when second of six at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) 11 days ago; should win races, but is up against a useful opponent in Romantic Symphony.
runners.comment: Second on her Southwell return 11 days ago; up against a useful rival in Romantic Symphony

runners.name: Romantic Symphony
runners.spotlight: Sister to four winners including Yibir (7f-2m including 2yo/US Grade 1; RPR 123) and Wild Illusion (1m/1m2f including 2yo/Group 1; 116); sent off 10-11 when getting the better of a subsequent winner on her Newmarket debut (1m, good) last September; bred to stay well and a major player on her return.
runners.comment: Justified odds of 10-11 on last September's Newmarket debut; major player on return

runners.name: Fashions Fancy
runners.spotlight: Dam Listed-placed 7f/1m winner (RPR 102), half-sister to winners Rebel's Romance (multiple 1m4f Group/Grade 1 including Breeders' Cup Turf) and Measured Time (UAE 1m1f Group 1/US 9.5f Grade 1), out of Listed-placed 7f 2yo winner; looks the part on breeding and yard has a great strike-rate with 3yo newcomers, but Romantic Symphony does looks the stable first string.
runners.comment: Looks the part on breeding, but Romantic Symphony does looks the stable first string.

runners.name: Light Of Paris
runners.spotlight: 500,000gns yearling; half-sister to seven winners including Lucky Lion (German 1m-1m2f including Group 1; RPR 117), Lips Freedom (1m1f-1m4f Flat including Listed; 108; 2m2f hurdle) and Lips Vega (German 1m; 102); dam German 6f-7f winner (inc 2yo/Listed; 102); appealing pedigree and stable can get one ready first time; market confidence would be significant.
runners.comment: Appealing pedigree and stable can get one ready first time; market confidence significant

runners.name: Aukus
runners.spotlight: 115,000gns yearling; sister to 1m 2yo/1m2f winner Herring Island (RPR 102), closely related to Group-placed 7f 2yo winner War Rooms (106), half-sister to two winners including Aduna (1m2f); dam twice-raced (61) half-sister to Dewhurst winner Distant Music; nice pedigree and stable can get them ready first time; market useful.
runners.comment: Nice pedigree and stable can get them ready first time; market useful

runners.name: Crimson Dawn
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way into last of eight in two runs on Polytrack (1m/7f) ten weeks apart; has undergone wind surgery since; no appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way into last of eight in two runs on Polytrack; no appeal.
runners.owner: Ben Pay And Carter Roofing Solutions Ltd

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div I)

runners.name: Go Rimbaud
runners.spotlight: Reached the frame in his first two starts in the autumn before a near-miss over 7f here (100-30) in November; his form has been consistent rather than progressive so far but he sets a fair standard and is respected back up in trip on his return.
runners.comment: Had a near-miss here (7f) in November and he's respected back up in trip on his return

runners.name: Turret
runners.spotlight: 580,000euros yearling; second foal of an unraced sister to Group 1 winners Hydrangea (1m/1m4f), The United States (Australian 1m2f) and Hermosa (1,000 Guineas/Irish 1,000 Guineas); another striking newcomer and the market should be informative.
runners.comment: 580,000gns yearling; another striking newcomer and the market should be informative

runners.name: Romanticizing
runners.spotlight: 180,000gns yearling; tenth foal; closely related to 7f/1m winner Firmament (including AW; RPR 115), half-brother to five winners including Seven Heavens (5f-7f including HK; 107) and You're Hired (7f-11.4f including AW; 103); dam 7f-1m1f winner (including Group 3; 115); drawn out wide on debut but he's bred to be smart and needs a close look.
runners.comment: Drawn out wide on debut but he's bred to be smart and needs a close look

runners.name: Besieged
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m winner (including Group 1 Falmouth Stakes; RPR 117), closely related to 2m winner First Emperor, half-sister to useful 1m winner Midnight Gun, out of 1,000 Guineas/Coronation Stakes winner Sky Lantern; interesting newcomer and he needs watching in market.
runners.comment: Out of Falmouth winner and he looks the part on paper; needs watching in market

runners.name: Mmmbop
runners.spotlight: 17,000gns foal, 20,000gns yearling, 7,000gns 2yo; fourth foal; closely related to winners Bowerchalke (1m-1m3f AW; RPR 93) and Alvediston (German/Belgian 7f-9.5f including AW); dam Listed-placed 7f 2yo/1m2f winner (92); this looks a tough starting point and yard is 0-9 in maidens in recent years.
runners.comment: This looks a tough starting point and yard is 0-9 in maidens in recent years

runners.name: Electric Dreams
runners.spotlight: 475,000gns yearling; brother to Group-placed 1m/1m2f AW winner Go Go Boots (RPR 103), half-brother to Group-placed 7f-1m5f winner Travolta (including AW/Australia; 108); dam 9.4f AW 2yo/11.6f turf Listed winner (104); lots to like on paper and interesting to see how he figures in market.
runners.comment: 475,000gns yearling; interesting newcomer and he needs watching in market

runners.name: Bumaan
runners.spotlight: Well held in two novice events (7f) last month, with RPRs in the 40s on both occasions; remains best watched for now.
runners.comment: Well held in two novice events (7f) last month, with RPRs in the 40s on both occasions

runners.name: Pace Attack
runners.spotlight: 36,000euros yearling, resold 50,000gns later in 2024; outsider on his C&D debut two weeks ago but he made some late headway and was only beaten around 4l; that was an encouraging start and he should know more this time; could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Outsider over C&D two weeks ago but he shaped with some promise; not ruled out

runners.name: Head Office
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; dam placed 6f AW (RPR 62), half-sister to useful winners Mister Snowdon (6f/7f) and Upward Spiral (5f GB 2yo/US 1m); yard 0-25 in maidens in recent years and she's best watched on bleated debut.
runners.comment: Yard 0-25 in maidens in recent years and she's best watched on belated debut

runners.name: Ocean Force
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-brother to winners Ocean Vision (5f-1m including AW/Listed; RPR 105), Sootability (7f-10.4f; 80) and Khawaater (6f AW 2yo; 69); dam Listed-placed 7f winner (100); makes belated debut as a 5yo and he's probably best watched from wide draw.
runners.comment: Has useful pedigree but he makes his belated debut as a 5yo; probably best watched

runners.name: The Mayor
runners.spotlight: Well-held fourth in sole point in May 2023 and he's not been seen since then; can only be watched back from a mammoth absence on stable debut.
runners.comment: Well held in sole point in May 2023; can only be watched after a mammoth absence

races.name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Maiden Stakes (GBB Race) (Div II)

runners.name: Domenico Contarini
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns yearling, 750,000gns breeze-up 2yo; sent off 7-4 when beaten a length into second of ten on his Newcastle debut (1m, Tapeta) in February, with 5l back to the third; holds some lofty entries including four at Group 1 level; high on list.
runners.comment: Beaten a length into second of ten on his Newcastle debut in February; high on list

runners.name: Kenergy
runners.spotlight: Sent off 80-1 for his debut over 7f here three weeks ago, but showed ability in finishing fourth of the eight runners; the extra furlong should suit and he makes the shortlist.
runners.comment: Showed ability when fourth on his debut over 7f here three weeks ago; shortlisted

runners.name: Zatsgood
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in two starts on the AW over 16 months apart, the latest when beaten 5l on stable debut at Newcastle (7f, Tapeta; 4-9) 18 days ago; extra furlong should help but will need to progress again to get the better of the 3yos.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two starts on the AW over 16 months apart; will need to progress again

runners.name: Heraldry
runners.spotlight: 11-2 when fifth of ten on his Newcastle debut (1m, Tapeta) in November; open to improvement and the stable won a division of this race last year.
runners.comment: Fifth on his Newcastle debut in November; stable won a division of this race last year

runners.name: Flying Squad
runners.spotlight: Sent off 40-1 for his Southwell debut (7f, Tapeta) in November, but showed promise in finishing fifth of 14 behind a nice prospect; the form of that race has worked out well; gelded since; worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Showed promise when fifth of 14 on his Southwell debut in November; watch market

runners.name: Get Outta Here
runners.spotlight: Finished down the field on his debut in a valuable contest for unraced 2yos at Deauville (7f, soft) last August; gelded since; half-brother to an AW winner; market helpful.
runners.comment: Down the field on his Deauville debut last August; market helpful

runners.name: Mick The Hat
runners.spotlight: Sent off 16-1 when a well-held fifth of seven on his Wolverhampton debut (8.6f, Tapeta) last month; may come into this own in middle-distance handicaps in due course.
runners.comment: Well-held on his Wolverhampton debut; may come into this own in middle-distance handicaps

runners.name: Trade Marked
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival in her first three starts and was beaten miles each time; cheekpieces given a go; no appeal.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival in first three starts and beaten miles each time; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Big Eagle
runners.spotlight: Beaten 15l when fifth of seven behind a 1-10 shot on his Haydock debut (1m, good to soft) in October; half-brother to a French AW winner, but improvement is needed.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l when fifth of seven on his Haydock debut in October; improvement is needed

runners.name: Master Dancer
runners.spotlight: Hurdle/chase winner, but sent off 400-1 when well beaten on his belated Flat debut over C&D in October; no appeal on first start since.
runners.comment: 400-1 when well beaten on his belated Flat debut over C&D in October; no appeal

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who is just 1-17, but he only failed by a nose to double his tally over C&D nine days ago; another big run seems likely from the same mark.
runners.comment: Failed by a nose to double his tally over C&D nine days ago; another big run seems likely

runners.name: Arantes Nascimento
runners.spotlight: Often placed but has never passed the post first in any of his 23 starts, his sole success courtesy of the stewards; return to this trip should suit, but others preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: Often placed but has never passed the post first in any of his 23 starts; others preferred

runners.name: Atmosphere
runners.spotlight: All seven starts on the AW have been here, with his record in handicaps reading 11332; drawn a bit wider than ideal and another furlong may suit ideally, but still high on the list in this field.
runners.comment: Record in handicaps here reads 11332; drawn wider than ideal but still high on the list

runners.name: Ash Wednesday
runners.spotlight: Last 11 AW starts have been on Tapeta (231115111852), but he was beaten less than a length into third of 14 on his last visit here (1m); back up 1lb for his latest second of 12 at Southwell (7f)), but still 1lb below his last winning mark; respected.
runners.comment: Better known for his Tapeta achievements, but ran well on his last visit here; respected

runners.name: Dancing Terror
runners.spotlight: First and second in two 7f novices at Lingfield the winter before last, but having been off for a year he failed to beat a rival on handicap debut there (1m; 40-1) in February; has something to prove.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival on handicap debut at Lingfield in February; has something to prove

runners.name: State Of Madness
runners.spotlight: Did well in seven starts for Charlie Clover after returning from six months off in October (1121432), but held on his debut for this yard here (6f) a week ago; has never attempted this far before.
runners.comment: Held on debut for this yard here a week ago and has never attempted this far before
runners.owner: The Likely Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: King Of Ithaca
runners.spotlight: Best known for his Southwell exploits, but has run well here including a win over this trip; the outside stall is not ideal, though.
runners.comment: Best known for his Southwell exploits, but has on over C&D; outside stall not ideal

runners.name: Miss Chester
runners.spotlight: Landed a maiden over C&D in May last year but her form has rather gone west since, albeit she wasn't beaten far into eighth of 12 here (1m) six weeks ago; others stronger nonetheless.
runners.comment: Landed a maiden over C&D in May last year but her form has rather gone west since

runners.name: Nubough
runners.spotlight: Capable on his day and 1lb below his last winning mark, but now at the veteran stage and recent form has an uneven look to it; others more compelling.
runners.comment: 1lb below his last winning mark, but recent form has an uneven look to it

runners.name: Jersey Maverick
runners.spotlight: Has run very well in his last two starts over C&D, only going down by a neck in October; not seen since, though, and may just need the run.
runners.comment: Not seen since beaten a neck over C&D in October; may just need the run
runners.owner: Tim Cottier Clark Spence And Fellowes

runners.name: The Bitter Moose
runners.spotlight: Returned to winning form in emphatic fashion at Epsom (7f, good) last August, but following an 8lb rise he failed to beat a rival in an amateurs' event at Ascot (7f, good to soft) in October; opposable on first start since.
runners.comment: Not seen since finishing last of 17 at Ascot in October; opposable

runners.name: Margorie
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced; won a Windsor novice (6f, soft; 40-1) last September, but finished around 9l behind Atmosphere on her handicap debut here (1m) the following month; down the list on her first start since.
runners.comment: Last seen finishing well behind Atmosphere here in October; down the list

races.name: Virgin Bet Daily Extra Places Fillies' Handicap (London Middle Distance Qualifier) (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Rastnet
runners.spotlight: Consistent filly who probably ran into one when second of seven at Newcastle (1m4f, Tapeta) 18 days ago; much respected now just up against her own sex.
runners.comment: Probably ran into one at Newcastle last time; much respected against her own sex

runners.name: Club Class
runners.spotlight: Consistent last year (3232312); easily landed a 1m4f novice in a first-time tongue-tie here in September and put up an even better effort when second of 13 in a handicap over the same trip the following month; has fitness to prove after 161 days off, but still a major player.
runners.comment: Ended last year with a win and a second over 1m4f here; major player if ready to go

runners.name: Schemaya
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) in May last year and ran her best race since when second of six at Wolverhampton (1m6f, Tapeta) in October; not quite sure how she will cope with the drop in trip after six months off.
runners.comment: Off six months since coming second over 1m6f at Wolverhampton; not sure how she will cope

runners.name: Caramay
runners.spotlight: Tough mare who has been in good form since December including a win over C&D in February; almost certain to run her race, but she looks on a stiff mark now and may need to give best to younger fillies.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but looks on a stiff mark now and may need to give best to younger fillies

runners.name: Dojin
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in all three starts on the AW including winning a Southwell novice (1m3f, Tapeta) in March last year, but was behind Spirit Of Jura on handicap debut there the following month; not seen since.
runners.comment: Behind Spirit Of Jura on handicap debut at Southwell a year ago and not seen since

runners.name: Spirit Of Jura
runners.spotlight: Won four times over 1m-1m5f last year, three of them on the AW; may be better for last month's Southwell return, but she looks weighted up to her best.
runners.comment: Won four times last year; may have needed latest run, but looks weighted up to her best

runners.name: Big Bear Hug
runners.spotlight: Made a successful stable debut after seven months off at Goodwood (1m4f, good to soft) in June last year, but is now 7lb lower for a reason; others stronger.
runners.comment: Made a successful stable debut at Goodwood last June, but is now 7lb lower for a reason

races.name: Every Saturday Money Back Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Arry Up
runners.spotlight: Got to the front from his wide stall and was a convincing C&D winner on seasonal debut a week ago; carries 6lb penalty in higher-grade race today but is better drawn this time and could be hard to catch.
runners.comment: Got to the front from wide stall and was C&D winner on seasonal debut a week ago

runners.name: Hallo Spaceboy
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 6f on turf before winning 5f AW novice at skinny odds at Newcastle in November; back up in trip for this seasonal/handicap debut and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Placed twice over 6f on turf before winning 5f AW novice at Newcastle in November

runners.name: Return Of The Gods
runners.spotlight: Closely related to smart AW performer Cool Hand Luke; built upon promising debut when leading close home at Wolverhampton (6f) in October; makes handicap debut after 170-day absence but should have more improvement to come; tongue-tie added.
runners.comment: Narrow winner of 6f Wolverhampton maiden in October, his second start; can improve again

runners.name: Night Mission
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all three 2yo races, finding one too good when 2-7 favourite for run-of-the-mill Redcar novice (6f, good) on most recent occasion; gelded during subsequent break; probably needs improvement on this handicap/AW debut.
runners.comment: Made the frame in all three 2yo races but probably needs improvement on handicap/AW debut

runners.name: Cryingintherain
runners.spotlight: Came good at the third attempt, keeping on well in 6f Lingfield novice in December; not seen since and not ideally drawn for handicap debut but opening mark ought to be workable.
runners.comment: Came good at the third attempt, keeping on well in 6f Lingfield novice in December

runners.name: Perfect Location
runners.spotlight: Won 6f AW maiden under Sean Levey at Southwell last summer and came second on nursery debut at Leicester (6f, good to soft) on latest outing in September; nicely drawn for a prominent racer here; contender if fully tuned up after 198-day break.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner last summer; second on nursery debut on latest outing in September

runners.name: Unionville
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish gelding who went close on stable/handicap debut over C&D in January and dead-heated in tactical Chelmsford maiden (6f) the following month; probably needs to find extra today but still considered.
runners.comment: Close second over C&D on stable/handicap debut and has dead-heated in a maiden since

runners.name: Goldwork
runners.spotlight: Gained his 2yo win at Goodwood (6f, good) last spring; struggled to cope with heavy ground on last summer's nursery debut and predictably found valuable sales race too hot when last in action but this contest looks more suitable on his seasonal return.
runners.comment: Goodwood winner last spring; last two 2yo defeats can be excused; makes reappearance here

races.name: Virgin Bet Handicap (London Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Initial Blue
runners.spotlight: Consistent gelding who is 7lb higher than when making all over C&D in February, but has been placed in all three starts off this sort of mark in the meantime; the outside stall is the main concern.
runners.comment: Placed in all three starts since making all over C&D; outside stall the main concern

runners.name: Teardrops
runners.spotlight: Last 11 AW starts have come at Newcastle including three wins over this trip; well beaten in both visits here, though they were at the start of his career; Oisin Murphy is 6-29 (21%) for the stable in the last five years.
runners.comment: Last 11 AW starts have come at Newcastle including three wins over this trip

runners.name: Just Jump
runners.spotlight: 1-20, the win coming in a Bellewstown maiden claimer (1m, soft) in October; has shown encouragement since joining Tony Carroll and can't be easily dismissed.
runners.comment: 1-20, but has shown encouragement since joining Tony Carroll; can't be easily dismissed

runners.name: Hello Luna
runners.spotlight: 2lb lower than when gaining her only success at Windsor (5f, good to firm) last June and has the inside stall, but she does seem best at the minimum trip.
runners.comment: 2lb lower than when gaining her only success last June, but does seem best at 5f

runners.name: Twilight Madness
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner and dual C&D winner who has been in good form lately, including scoring by a neck at Bath (5f, good) on Sunday; has a 4lb penalty for that, but is well drawn to attack and much respected.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner who won at Bath on Sunday; well drawn to attack
runners.owner: P R Hedger & P C F Racing Ltd

runners.name: Hint Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Appears to have appreciated the return to 7f with wins at Southwell (Tapeta) and Chelmsford (Polytrack) on his last two starts; up another 3lb, but bigger questions may be the return to 6f and his high draw.
runners.comment: Bids for a hat-trick from 3lb higher, but the return to 6f and a high draw pose questions

runners.name: Vitalline
runners.spotlight: Frequent slow-starter who is a four-time winner here, the latest in a 0-50 classified event over 7f last month; twice held since, though, and doesn't have a great draw.
runners.comment: Four-time winner here, but well held in last two starts and drawn wide

runners.name: Big Bard
runners.spotlight: Consistency not his strong suit, but he is 2-5 over C&D and latest heavy defeat excusable on account of his wide draw; capable if on a going day.
runners.comment: Consistency not his strong suit, but he is 2-5 over C&D; capable if on a going day

runners.name: Noble Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who has gone backwards since an encouraging 2yo debut for Andrew Balding; best watched.
runners.comment: Ten-race maiden who has gone backwards since an encouraging 2yo debut for Andrew Balding

runners.name: Travel Agent
runners.spotlight: Won at Lingfield (6f, Polytrack) for Martin Dunne last summer and made an encouraging stable debut after six months off when fourth of nine at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) 13 days ago; could go well if building on that with an eyeshield given a go.
runners.comment: Made an encouraging stable debut when fourth at Southwell 13 days ago; eyeshield on

runners.name: Watermelon Sugar
runners.spotlight: In good form at Lingfield lately and has become a bit of a specialist over 6f there (112121); it's a different story here (8770) and that would be the concern.
runners.comment: Has a fine record over 6f at Lingfield, but it's a different story here

runners.name: Arnaz
runners.spotlight: 8lb lower than when second of nine over C&D last September, but not built on it (mainly over further) since; return to this trip may help, but he is 0-17 since his successful handicap debut.
runners.comment: Return to this trip may help, but he is 0-17 since his successful handicap debut

races.name: Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Fiddlers Green
runners.spotlight: May have done too much early and might have failed for stamina when sixth of eight at Chelmsford (13.3f. Polytrack) 13 days ago (Take The Boat second); better judged on his previous form around here including wins over 1m3f/1m4f; respected for stable that also runs Maywedance.
runners.comment: Did too much early last time, but has winning form over 1m3f/1m4f around here; respected
runners.owner: Phil Hart And The Cross Racing Club

runners.name: Take The Boat
runners.spotlight: Three of her four wins have come over 7f/1m here, but she stays well as she showed when scoring at Wolverhampton (1m4f, Tapeta) in February; may have been unlucky not to win when beaten a short head at Chelmsford (13.3f, Polytrack) 13 days ago and one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Three of her four wins have come here; may have been unlucky last time; shortlisted

runners.name: Iwantmytimewithyou
runners.spotlight: Gained back-to-back successes at Ripon (1m4f, good) and Bath (1m6f, firm) last July, the latter courtesy of the stewards; lost his form aftewards and has something to prove on his Polytrack debut after seven months off.
runners.comment: Two wins on turf last summer; something to prove on Polytrack debut after seven months off

runners.name: Maywedance
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form of late, but is 1lb lower than when dead-heating over C&D in November on her final start for Alice Haynes; possibilities if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 1lb lower than when dead-heating over C&D in November; possibilities if bouncing back

runners.name: Fravanco
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen over hurdles lately and is just 2-33 under rules, but one of his wins came over C&D and his last few efforts on the AW off similar marks would give him an each-way chance here.
runners.comment: Just 2-33 under rules but one of his wins came over C&D; each-way chance

runners.name: Dotties Star
runners.spotlight: Better known as a hurdler these days, but he made a successful return to the Flat when narrowly landing a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield (1m4f, Polytrack) a fortnight ago; this will be harder, but he remains unexposed on the AW.
runners.comment: Narrowly won a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield a fortnight ago; still unexposed on AW

runners.name: Upepo
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent, but has won six times on the AW including five over 1m4f/1m5f at Lingfield; has run well here, but stall 13 isn't ideal even over this trip.
runners.comment: Not the most consistent; has run well here, but stall 13 isn't ideal even over this trip

runners.name: Pablo Prince
runners.spotlight: Has gone close a couple of times in recent outings, but has only managed to win one of his last 28 starts; likely to find one or two too good.
runners.comment: Has only managed to win one of his last 28 starts; likely to find one or two too good

runners.name: Pershalla
runners.spotlight: 0-5; made the frame in all three starts at Lingfield (1m/1m2f), but having been off for 11 months she failed to beat a rival on handicap debut over 1m3f here five weeks ago; hood will need to make a difference.
runners.comment: 0-5; failed to beat a rival on handicap debut over 1m3f here five weeks ago; hood on

runners.name: Uzincso
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has nothing to prove on this track having won seven times over 1m/1m3f here; can go well fresh so the four-month absence shouldn't be a problem, but the trip remains a moot point.
runners.comment: Has won seven times over 1m/1m3f here; can go well fresh but the trip remains a moot point

runners.name: Dubai Immo
runners.spotlight: 3-41; 7lb lower than when making a successful reappearance at Chelmsford (1m2f, Polytrack) around this time last year and he has offered a little bit of encouragement in his last couple of outings; off 11 weeks, but could go well at a price.
runners.comment: 7lb below last winning mark; off 11 weeks, but could go well at a price

runners.name: Mrembo
runners.spotlight: Five-time winner over 1m2f-11.7f on turf, but out of the frame in three goes on the AW; not seen in six months and has no great record fresh.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three goes on the AW; off six months and has no great record fresh

races.name: Cochrane Handicap (Class 5) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Always My Folks
runners.spotlight: 0-16, but in the frame a few times here as when beaten 2l into fourth over 5f four weeks ago; frame material again with Zac Purton retaining the ride.
runners.comment: 0-16, but in the frame on a few occasions here including last time; frame material again

runners.name: Team Happy
runners.spotlight: 2-28 with both wins (including one over C&D) in the autumn of 2024; more out than in since and makes his stable debut after 48 days off; latest trial nothing to write home about; usual tongue-tie missing.
runners.comment: 2-28 with both wins in the autumn of 2024; more out than in since; stable debut

runners.name: Turf Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Reserve; useful 2yo for Andrew Balding in Britain, but has shown little in 16 local starts; makes stable debut after ten weeks off; third in a barrier trial here last month, but still makes limited appeal.
runners.comment: Useful 2yo for Andrew Balding, but has shown little in 16 local starts; limited appeal
runners.owner: Dr John Shum Ding Ping & Dr Lawrence Chan Pui Wing

runners.name: Chill Master
runners.spotlight: 0-11, but was only beaten around half a length into fourth over C&D in February and has since finished runner-up in a barrier trial; better drawn this time and respected.
runners.comment: 0-11, but close fourth over C&D in February and has since finished runner-up in a trial
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Francis Lee Bing Kwong & Yves Lee Kun Fun

runners.name: Nebraskan
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 2-2 in Australia and 0-29 in Hong Kong, but in the frame on 11 occasions; often makes the running; each-way claims again if getting a run.
runners.comment: 0-29 in Hong Kong, but in the frame 11 times; each-way claims again if getting a run
runners.owner: Kenneth Lam Sze Ken Timothy Junior Lam & T Fok Kwong Hang

runners.name: Concorde Star
runners.spotlight: First and second over C&D either side of New Year, but only seventh in his last two starts; much better drawn than has been the case for the majority of his recent outings, though; blinkers return; major player.
runners.comment: First and second over C&D either side of New Year; well drawn this time; major player
runners.owner: Philip Yu Chi Wai & Dr Jennie Peterson Yu

runners.name: Sparkle And Gold
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in 11 starts at Sha Tin, but not beaten far into fifth when dropped to Class 5 on penultimate start and was drawn widest last time; cheekpieces removed; each-way possiblities.
runners.comment: 0-11 at Sha Tin but not beaten far into fifth when dropped to Class 5 on penultimate start

runners.name: Rewarding Twinkle
runners.spotlight: 0-16, but has made the frame a few times including over C&D; another placing may be his best hope.
runners.comment: 0-16, but has made the frame a few times; another placing may be his best hope

runners.name: Thousand Cups
runners.spotlight: Handy sort who is 0-11 and last two efforts in Class 5 have been modest, but he finished third in a Class 4 over C&D in December; could go well if given another positive ride from his low draw.
runners.comment: 0-11, but finished third in a Class 4 over C&D in December; could go well from a low draw
runners.owner: Tsang Kam Chuen Simon Siu Chak Yu Et Al

runners.name: Legendary Impact
runners.spotlight: 1-5 in New Zealand, but out of the frame in 13 starts in Hong Kong; another drop in grade the one ray of hope; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in 13 starts in Hong Kong; another drop in grade the one ray of hope

runners.name: Decision Link
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over 6f/7f at Sha Tin; drop in grade a positive, but the outside stall isn't; first-time visor.
runners.comment: 0-8 over at Sha Tin; drop in grade a positive, but the outside stall isn't; visor on
runners.owner: Mixion Lam Ting Kwok & Luo Hong Fen

runners.name: Podium
runners.spotlight: Just 2-34 in Hong Kong albeit both wins have come over C&D, the latest in November; capable of playing a part on his best form, but stall 11 complicates matters.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner; capable of playing a part on his best form, but drawn wide

runners.name: One And Only
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in seven starts including when dropped to Class 5 at Sha Tin (first-time blinkers) last time; makes stable debut after three months off; second in one of three barrier trials since, but others are more solid.
runners.comment: Well beaten in seven starts; makes stable debut after three months off

runners.name: Blue Baron
runners.spotlight: Made the frame three times at Sha Tin, but now 0-11 and the drop to Class 5 made no difference last time; makes stable debut after five months off; placed in three of his four barrier trials since, but more needed.
runners.comment: 0-11 and drop to Class 5 made no difference last time; stable debut after five months off
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Mark Fong Chung Matthew Fong & Victoria Fong

races.name: D'Aguilar Handicap (Class 4) (Course C+3) (Turf) (Div I)

runners.name: Super Goldendragon
runners.spotlight: Well held in four starts over 1m1f/1m2f at Sha Tin; improvement needed with blinkers replacing cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Well held in four starts at Sha Tin; improvement needed; blinkers on
runners.owner: Yip Shu Bun & Jonathan Chok Shee Feng

runners.name: Aestheticism
runners.spotlight: Four wins, the latest in a Class 3 over C&D a year ago; not operating at the same level since, though, and the draw hasn't been kind; first-time eyeshield.
runners.comment: Won a Class 3 over C&D a year ago, but not operating at the same level since; eyeshield on

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: Reserve; all seven wins have come over over C&D, the latest in September; sliipping back down the weights and most of his recent efforts can be forgiven on account of the draw; possibilities if getting a run.
runners.comment: All seven wins have come over over C&D; slipping back down the weights; a possible
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Run Run Timing
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 2-30, but has finished first and second under positive rides over C&D in his last two starts (had two of these behind him last time); one for the shortlist if getting a run.
runners.comment: 2-30, but has finished first and second under positive rides over C&D in last two starts
runners.owner: Tang Ming Tang Chak Leung Et Al

runners.name: Vivacious Win
runners.spotlight: 0-8, but his record over C&D reads 4224, his latest effort even better than it looks given he had the outside stall; major player.
runners.comment: 0-8, but record over C&D reads 4224, his latest effort even better than it looks; player

runners.name: Precision Hope
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in five starts, the latest over C&D; hard to recommend from his high draw.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts, the latest over C&D; hard to recommend from his high draw

runners.name: Yiu Cheung Victory
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season including one over C&D; not the most consistent, but he finished a close fifth here a fortnight ago with two of these behind; well drawn and could go well if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: C&D winner; inconsistent, but well drawn and could go well if putting best foot forward

runners.name: Beauty Viva
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in Britain and made it 20th-time lucky in Hong Kong when successful over C&D a fortnight ago; 6lb rise will demand more, but still one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Won over C&D a fortnight ago; 6lb rise will demand more, but still one for the shortlist

runners.name: Golden Darci
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner here last season, but last four efforts have been modest and a step back up in trip isn't sure to suit after 112 days off; placed in one of three subsequent trials, but other more convincing.
runners.comment: Dual 6f winner; last four efforts have been modest; step back up in trip not sure to suit

runners.name: General Redwood
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all three starts, the last two over C&D; off ten weeks, but has been placed in both trials in the meantime and remains unexposed; could step up.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all three starts, but placed in both trials since; could step up

runners.name: Fortune Star
runners.spotlight: 1-13, the win coming over C&D in January; finished 3l behind Beauty Viva here a fortnight ago, but is now 7lb better off; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: 3l behind Beauty Viva here a fortnight ago, but is now 7lb better off; shortlisted
runners.owner: Kelvin Chan Kam Kei & Eddie Chan Kam Kong

runners.name: Vermilion Tempest
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in New Zealand, but out of the frame in five starts in Hong Kong; drawn widest when sixth over C&D three weeks ago (Aestheticism fourth), but still needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts in Hong Kong; needs to take a big step forward

runners.name: Celtic Times
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season, the latest in January; poorly drawn in his next two starts, but ran better when fifth four weeks ago; could go well from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season, the latest in January; could go well from the inside stall

runners.name: Osi Honour
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Australia, but out of the frame in five starts in Hong Kong including when dropped to Class 4 at Sha Tin (1m1f, dirt) a week ago; much more needed returned to turf.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in five starts in Hong Kong; much more needed returned to turf
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Philip Ng Yuk Wing & Michael Ng Mun Kit

races.name: Graham Handicap (Class 4) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Golden Friendship
runners.spotlight: First and second in two starts at Class 5 level over C&D, but out of the frame in 13 outings in Class 4 company; has finished behind Beauty Thunder on all four occasions they have met.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but out of the frame in 13 outings in Class 4 company
runners.owner: Janet Wong Hoi Shan John Yuen Se Kit Et Al

runners.name: Happy United
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 5f/6f here; losing run now up to 23 and he has a bit of ground to make up with Beauty Thunder on recent meetings, but he has a good draw; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner; losing run up to 23, but well drawn and holds each-way claims

runners.name: Forza Leader
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Australia but little show in seven starts over 5f/6f on turf/dirt in Hong Kong; well drawn, but needs more in the returning tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Little show in seven starts in Hong Kong; needs more in the returning tongue-tie

runners.name: Get Friendly
runners.spotlight: Finished third on his debut at Sha Tin (5f) in November, but his form has regressed in four outings since; well drawn, but he needs the blinkers to help halt the slide.
runners.comment: Third on his debut at Sha Tin in November, but his form has regressed since; blinkers on

runners.name: Elegant Life
runners.spotlight: 0-17 at Sha Tin but 1-2 at Happy Valley, the win coming in a Class 5 over this trip on his penultimate start; third back in a Class 4 at Sha Tin (6f) since and interesting back here despite not the best of draws.
runners.comment: 1-2 at Happy Valley, the win coming in a Class 5 over this trip on penultimate start

runners.name: Zeta Hedge
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who showed plenty of promise when runner-up on his debut over C&D in October, but has failed to beat a rival in three outings since; again doesn't have a great draw.
runners.comment: Second on debut over C&D in October, but has failed to beat a rival in three starts since

runners.name: Superb King
runners.spotlight: Handy sort; 0-11 including ten local starts; twice runner-up over 6f here this year including three weeks ago; a player if not inconvenienced by the return to 5f.
runners.comment: Twice runner-up over 6f here this year; a player if not inconvenienced by the return to 5f

runners.name: Beauty Thunder
runners.spotlight: Record over C&D reads 412222; has been done no favours by the draw, but he was also drawn wide in his last two starts and that didn't stop him from running very well; respected.
runners.comment: Record over C&D reads 412222; drawn wide again, but still respected

runners.name: Joy Star
runners.spotlight: Has hinted at a small amount of ability in three starts over 5f/6f on turf/dirt at Sha Tin, despite finishing out of the frame; still needs to find more, though.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in three starts at Sha Tin; showed a little ability but still needs more
runners.owner: Lam King Wai & Dr & Mrs Lam Kai Cheong

runners.name: Viva Boss
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all four starts this year and will do well to break his duck from the outside stall; tongue-tie given a go.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all four starts and drawn widest today; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Turbo Jefferies
runners.spotlight: Reserve; finished in midfield on his official debut at Sha Tin (5f) ten days ago and will need to improve on that.
runners.comment: Finished in midfield on his Sha Tin debut ten days ago and will need to improve on that
runners.owner: Simmy Leung Jan Lai et al

runners.name: Beauty Show
runners.spotlight: Won the latest of his four trials over C&D under Zac Purton and interesting with him retaining the ride for this official debut.
runners.comment: Won his latest trial over C&D under Zac Purton; interesting with him retaining the ride

runners.name: Tycoon Resources
runners.spotlight: Has won the last two of his seven trials, including one over C&D; worth a second look on this official debut.
runners.comment: Has won his last two trials, including one over C&D; worth a second look on official debut

runners.name: Healthy Healthy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; losing run up to 20 and well held at Sha Tin 102 days ago, but was running well around here prior to that; fair fourth in latest trial and drops back into Class 4 company; possibilities if getting a run.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 20; fourth in latest trial and drops back to Class 4 level; possibilities
runners.owner: Lo Kui Lun Lo Hoe Et Al

races.name: D'Aguilar Handicap (Class 4) (Course C+3) (Turf) (Div II)

runners.name: Made For Life
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and hasn't kicked on from a promising debut at Sha Tin (6f) in November; much better drawn than he has been in his last three outings, though, so he could show a bit more.
runners.comment: Much better drawn than he has been in his last three outings; could show a bit more

runners.name: Winning Data
runners.spotlight: All three wins at Sha Tin (7f/1m), but he ran very well when beaten around half a length into third behind Golden Brilliant over C&D four weeks ago with four of these further behind; 5lb better off with the winner, but the outside stall dampens enthusiasm.
runners.comment: Half a length behind Golden Brilliant here last time and 5lb better off, but drawn widest
runners.owner: Dr Steven Li Siu Lung Cheng Lap Yin Et Al

runners.name: Amazing Award
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner early last year and has run well back here in his last two starts, finishing behind a couple of these when fourth four weeks ago; likely to go well again.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner early last year and has run well in his last two starts; can go well again
runners.owner: Chan Kwok Yuen Terence Lun Tin Yeu & Paul Li Cheuk Wai

runners.name: The Azure
runners.spotlight: Reserve; three-time C&D winner and 5lb lower than when scoring by a nose just under a year ago, but out of the frame in all nine starts since; could bounce back at some point and not dismissed if getting a run.
runners.comment: 5lb lower than when winning narrowly over C&D just under a year ago; could bounce back
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Alan Ho Pui Lun & Mr & Mrs Norman Tang Fai Ng

runners.name: Excellent Boy
runners.spotlight: 0-6, but was having his first start here when beaten under a length over C&D a fortnight ago; 1lb higher, but has more upside than many and a player if stepping up again.
runners.comment: 0-6, but was beaten under a length over C&D a fortnight ago; a player if stepping up again

runners.name: Mighty Steed
runners.spotlight: 1-20 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D from a high draw in October; placed on a couple of occasions in Class 3 company after that, but was a bit disappointing back at this level three weeks ago; a player if bouncing back with Zac Purton keeping the ride.
runners.comment: C&D winner in October but disappointing back in Class 4 last time; player if bouncing back

runners.name: Incanto Star
runners.spotlight: 0-6, but placed over C&D either side of New Year; needs to put a lesser effort in first-time blinkers (retained) last time behind him, but his latest trial gave some cause for optimism.
runners.comment: 0-6, but placed over C&D either side of New Year; ran well in latest trial

runners.name: Harmony Galaxy
runners.spotlight: Reserve; both wins in Hong Kong came over 1m1f here a year ago; 7lb lower than for the latest and he ran well when beaten less than 2l into third from the outside stall over C&D a fortnight ago; a player of getting a run.
runners.comment: 7lb below last winning mark; beaten under 2l into third over C&D a fortnight ago; player

runners.name: Golden Brilliant
runners.spotlight: Improved for the fitting of blinkers and off the mark at the seventh attempt when scoring by a neck over C&D four weeks ago with five of these behind; up 6lb, but still open to further progress; respected.
runners.comment: Won at the seventh attempt over C&D four weeks ago with five of these behind; respected

runners.name: Whowrotethatsong
runners.spotlight: Well held in three starts over 6f/7f here and at Sha Tin; needs a lot more raised again in trip.
runners.comment: Well held in three starts here and at Sha Tin; needs a lot more raised again in trip

runners.name: All Round Winner
runners.spotlight: 1-15 in Hong Kong, the win coming over 7f at Sha Tin in January; still 5lb higher and one previous attempt over this far proved inconclusive as regards stamina.
runners.comment: Still 5lb above last winnig mark and one previous go over this far proved inconclusive
runners.owner: Clement Chan Kam Wing Maggie Ho Sze Nga Et Al

runners.name: King Of Selection
runners.spotlight: 1-2 in New Zealand, but has blown out in five starts in Hong Kong including when dropped to this grade over C&D four weeks ago (behind five of these); his good draw the only obvious positive.
runners.comment: Has blown out in five starts in Hong Kong; his good draw the only obvious positive

runners.name: Automated
runners.spotlight: 1-1 in Australia, but has not yet shown a great deal in six starts in Hong Kong and is again drawn wide; needs the addition of a hood to make a difference.
runners.comment: Has not yet shown a great deal in six starts in Hong Kong and is again drawn wide; hood on

runners.name: Smiling One
runners.spotlight: Beaten a long way in two starts at Sha Tin (7f) last year and needs to fare better than in his two subsequent trials; new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way in two starts at Sha Tin last year; new headgear combination

races.name: Sports Club Diamond Jubilee Challenge Cup (Class 4) (Handicap) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Same To You
runners.spotlight: Has been a bit up and down since his successful seasonal debut at Sha Tin in September (6f), but did well to make the frame here last time from an unpromising position.
runners.comment: He did well to make the frame here last time from an unpromising position
runners.owner: Colin Cheung Kwok Lam Amos Cheung Sai Keung & Gary H K Yau

runners.name: Free Pony
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in December off only 1lb lower and latest third here was his best run for a while; armed with a good draw he should run well.
runners.comment: C&D winner in December off 1lb lower and latest third here was his best run for a while
runners.owner: Edwin Fong Yick Chung Edmund Ho Kwok Shing & So Wai Keung

runners.name: Forerunner
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-32 so he's exposed; however, he was a very close second over C&D four weeks ago and is only 2lb higher now.
runners.comment: Exposed but was a very close second over C&D four weeks ago and only 2lb higher now
runners.owner: Ng Tai Wing Anson Or Yiu Wah & Henry Yeung Mun Kin

runners.name: Smart Fighter
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back C&D wins last year, the latest off this mark; just one effort of note in five runs after a lengthy break but that was here and Zac Purton now takes over.
runners.comment: One effort of note in five runs after a break but that was here and Zac Purton rides
runners.owner: The Executors Of The Estate Of The Late Joseph Chung

runners.name: Star Figure
runners.spotlight: No wins in seven and has been getting going too late in his races, from 6f to 1m; may need further ideally but would have a chance if ridden closer to the pace; Zac Purton has been aboard the last twice at Sha Tin but not here.
runners.comment: No wins in seven and has been getting going too late in his races, from 6f to 1m

runners.name: Bright Day
runners.spotlight: Hardly progressive and his only win in 19 came at 7f, which seems more his trip; Sha Tin regular who hasn't shone on three previous visits here.
runners.comment: Hardly progressive and his only win in 19 came at 7f, which seems more his trip

runners.name: Flying Christie
runners.spotlight: Well down the field in all his official races and very opposable at the minute.
runners.comment: Well down the field in all his official races and very opposable at the minute

runners.name: Aquaman
runners.spotlight: Reserve; his third over 7f at Sha Tin two runs back is his standout piece of form; could be vulnerable back at 6f.
runners.comment: His third over 7f at Sha Tin two runs back is his standout piece of form
runners.owner: Steve Chan Chi Fung & Terry Ng Ting Chi

runners.name: Marvel And Gold
runners.spotlight: 2-2 in New Zealand but struggling in Hong Kong and was behind Free Pony the last time.
runners.comment: 2-2 in New Zealand but struggling in Hong Kong and was behind Free Pony the last time

runners.name: Monarch County
runners.spotlight: Dual winner, once over C&D; last four runs have been over 7f at Sha Tin and had a poor draw when below par last time; needs considering under Joao Moreira.
runners.comment: Dual winner, once over C&D; consistent and was hampered by a wide draw last time
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo Alex Fan Chen Yen Et Al

runners.name: A Time For Us
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia; Class 3s were too tough but was close up at this lower level over C&D two weeks ago; drawn 2 that day but now breaks from stall 10, which asks questions of him.
runners.comment: Appreciated the drop to a Class 4 last time but now has a wider draw to contend with

runners.name: Joker Orbit
runners.spotlight: Won in Australia and best run yet in Hong Kong when a closing fourth over C&D four weeks ago; however, he was no threat to the principals that day and needs more; down 2lb.
runners.comment: Fourth here last time but was no threat to the principals that day
runners.owner: Tobias Chong Wing To & Ophelia Chong Kar Wing

runners.name: Northern Fire Ball
runners.spotlight: Reserve; all three wins have been over 6f here and was twice close up at this track before an excusable defeat back over 5f at Sha Tin.
runners.comment: Twice close up at this track before an excusable defeat back over 5f at Sha Tin

runners.name: Find My Love
runners.spotlight: Has edged his way down to a good mark and was close up on his last visit here (penultimate run); well exposed but has each-way claims.
runners.comment: Has edged his way down to a good mark and was close up on his last visit here
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Dannis Lee Cheuk Yin

races.name: Wyndham Handicap (Class 4) (Course C+3) (Turf)

runners.name: Gameplayer Elite
runners.spotlight: A winner in Brazil; finally came good in Hong Kong when justifying favouritism over C&D a month ago, the form of which has been franked; up 6lb but raced prominently last time and he's well drawn to employ similar tactics.
runners.comment: Finally came good in Hong Kong when justifying favouritism over C&D a month ago; up 6lb
runners.owner: Lam Yau Pik Rex Lam Lam & Monica Lam Mung Ni

runners.name: Bigtime Generation
runners.spotlight: The best of his eight runs and by some way was the near miss over Sha Tin's 6f in January; hasn't kicked on.
runners.comment: Best run by some way was the near miss over Sha Tin's 6f in January; hasn't kicked on

runners.name: Tactical Command
runners.spotlight: Five wins include four over C&D, the latest off 1lb higher last July; runner-up here two starts back and his efforts either side can be excused on account of the draw; considered.
runners.comment: Second here two starts back and efforts either side can be excused on account of the draw

runners.name: Lucky McQueen
runners.spotlight: Off the mark over C&D four weeks ago and did that cosily despite a wide draw; exits from a similar stall and has shown in the past that he can deal with this 6lb higher mark; respected.
runners.comment: Off the mark over C&D four weeks ago and did that cosily despite a wide draw

runners.name: Grand Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced but well beaten behind Sky Cap here last time in this new headgear.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced but well beaten behind Sky Cap here last time in this new headgear

runners.name: Flash Star
runners.spotlight: C&D winner in December and hasn't been dropping lucky with the draw since then; stall 6 isn't a killer this time, though he did finish last in a recent trial.
runners.comment: C&D winner in December and hasn't been dropping lucky with the draw since then

runners.name: Awesome Treasure
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner but 0-3 for current yard and couldn't cope with a bad draw here last time; again poorly berthed.
runners.comment: Four-time winner but 0-3 for current yard and couldn't cope with a bad draw here last time

runners.name: Sky Cap
runners.spotlight: Held on narrowly over C&D three weeks ago and remains well handicapped on older form; none too consistent but he's very capable.
runners.comment: Held on narrowly over C&D three weeks ago and remains well handicapped on older form

runners.name: Happy Brethren
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in trials and in both official races at Sha Tin, looking sure to win last time over 6f until overhauled late on; same mark here and he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: Lightly raced and looked sure to win last time at Sha Tin until overhauled late on

runners.name: Glaciated
runners.spotlight: A winner over this trip at Sha Tin in December and since a close third there off this mark; last run was disappointing but has won a trial subsequently; however, he's unraced at Happy Valley and has dropped unlucky with the draw.
runners.comment: Sha Tin winner; he's unraced at Happy Valley and has dropped unlucky with the draw
runners.owner: Alan Chiu Chun Ming Sandy Cheng Chuk Wah & W Law Chi Yin

runners.name: Falcon Hunter
runners.spotlight: Huge odds when an eyecatching third over C&D 56 days ago when plenty went wrong; could be on the upgrade.
runners.comment: Huge odds when an eyecatching third over C&D 56 days ago when plenty went wrong

runners.name: Crossborderdude
